Working at Ardonagh Specialty means you’ll be part of The Ardonagh Group. We are proud of our innovative environment offering many opportunities for growth across the wider group. Employees regularly move between our united teams, and we encourage you to make your role your own.

Our offices are lively and exciting places to be, but we understand that life needs flexibility, and offer a genuinely flexible approach to working. If you are looking to join a thriving, energetic business with exciting plans, this role could be an ideal fit for you.

What We Can Offer

We offer much more than just an inclusive culture with apprenticeships, study support, participation in our annual Spotlight Awards, Community Trust, Sports Teams, office socials, events and so much more. All of this with a supportive management team as well as working alongside some of the industry’s top talent.

You’ll have access to wellbeing programs, fantastic discounts across many big-name businesses including supermarkets, gym memberships, restaurants, and healthcare cash plans etc.

Further Perks Of Working With Us (Fixed Benefits)

  • Employer pension contribution of 10% (providing you, the Employee provides 5%).
  • Good work life balance - flexibility to suit you.
  • Competitive salary.
  • Life Assurance at X4 of your base salary.
  • Group Income Protection.
  • Generous Annual Leave entitlement.
  • Private Medical Insurance.
  • Group annual bonus scheme.

Key Internal Relationships

Business leaders and C-Suite Executive

Ardonagh Portfolio Solutions (APS)

Finance Department

Marketing and Comms Department

Operational Management

MI team

Broking teams

Data visualisation

Data Engineering and Analytics teams

Compliance reporting

Key External Relationships

Provide reporting and analytical content to internal Operations, Clients and Underwriters

Purpose Of The Role

To support the Broking business through the development of high-quality information resources, and the delivery and the creation of presentations/whitepapers/survey insights to suit both internal stakeholders and external audiences.

Assist revenue production, client relationships, new business opportunities and drive business process innovation.

Key Role Accountabilities

Awareness of the reporting deliverables required from the business & operations leaders and the proactive & timely delivery of such

Understand the data contained within the PAS, its use by the business and the end-to-end data journey and capture

Identify deficiencies in source data and assist with resolution

Liaison with clients, underwriters and Brokers

Ability to build and crate content that that is fit for internal and external audiences. Ability to weave a compelling story using design, content and visualisation towards the creation of RFPs, proposals and Client presentations.

Data Engineering to ingest, cleanse and collate data – ability to work with large amounts of data in an agile manner,

Creation of reports and analytics

Presentation of the reports to support business development

Work with finance and MI on annual budgeting

Working on backlog of specified work as part of an agile delivery team

Qualifications & Experience

Educated to degree level in a relevant field or equivalent experience

At least 2 years of experience as a Data Analyst

Experience in insights and content creation.

Experience working in the Insurance Sector in a data-oriented role is essential, preferably with London Market Broking exposure.

Skills

Ability to understand data and translate this into meaningful business insights, reports and visualisations

Qlik developer skills (QlikView. QlikSense)

PowerBI

PowerBI Report Builder / Reporting Services

Building Data maps

Data modelling

Experience of software development lifecycle, working as a part of a team

Azure Data Factory

Insurance Sector domain knowledge

Analysis Services

Financial Reporting

Exposure to Agile development DevOps including automation of build and release

Experience on cloud tech (preferably Azure)

Experience within London Market Insurance & Reinsurance environment

Experience in statistical analysis or Data Science

Python or R coding skills

Person Specification

An enthusiastic Research and Data Analyst, able to understand complex data, able to work closely with IT development resources and business-people. Able to bring together different ideas and technology to help build successful IT solutions. Able to exploit data and to present this in intuitive and appealing ways to derive benefits for all stakeholders. Experience of building successful relationships with senior business stakeholders, offshore delivery partners. Proactive & ability to plan and manage multiple priorities Good knowledge of IT architecture and software development lifecycles. Hands-on development skills in data related and reporting technologies, plus the ability to adapt an acquire new skills. PowerBI and advanced excel skills are essential.

Think you don’t meet every requirement?

We are dedicated to creating a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace where everyone can thrive . If you’re excited about this role, but your experience doesn’t perfectly match what we are looking for, please apply anyway. You might just be the right fit for the job, or other opportunities we may have within the wider Group.

Interview Process

At Ardonagh Specialty, we have a straightforward interview process to ensure the best fit for both you and the company:

  • Submit your application with your CV, emphasising your skills and experience related to the job.
  • Our Talent Acquisition team will then arrange a call where they can tell you more about the role and the team. We want to learn about your motivation and goals, what you can bring to Ardonagh Specialty, and answer any immediate questions.
  • If successful, you will generally be invited to engage in a 1-hour interview with the Hiring Manager and selected team members, via teams or in person. We will discuss our technologies, key skills, and team dynamics and provide further opportunity to ask any questions. This is your chance to voice your thoughts on the role and we will outline our expectations and ensure this role aligns with your ambitions for the future.
  • Depending on the role, you may be invited to attend a second stage interview with further members of the team.

If successful or unsuccessful, we always try and move quickly in presenting an offer or providing feedback.

We truly value the diversity of our teams and as a Group, we are committed to supporting and welcoming individuals from all backgrounds, as we appreciate every perspective is a significant part of our success. Should you require any reasonable adjustment throughout the recruitment process, please do not hesitate to let a member of the Talent team know.

#AS

Title: Data Analyst – Band F
Place: Richmond Upon Thames, England, United Kingdom
Description:
As the living, growing home of our national story, The National Archives is already a special place to work. We’re an institution nearly 200 years old with a collection spanning 1,000 years of history. But it’s where we go next that makes things really interesting.

In our strategic vision: Archives for Everyone, we set ourselves the challenge of becoming the 21st Century national archive - a different kind of cultural and heritage institution: Inclusive, Entrepreneurial, Disruptive. We won’t become this overnight. It will take time, focus, effort and daring.

That’s where you come in. Because we can’t do this without you.

Job Overview

Salary: £41,000 per annum

Contract type: Fixed Term Appointment for 1 year

Band: F / SEO

Closing date: Thursday 29th May at midnight

This is a great opportunity to join The National Archives at an exciting point in the development of Manage Records Compliance, a new digital service with real strategic importance across government. The role offers the chance to combine data analysis, reporting and digital innovation to support better oversight of public records compliance, helping public bodies make informed decisions and improve their information management. It is an especially rewarding post for someone who wants to apply analytical skills to a high-profile public service mission and contribute to a service that will have lasting impact.

The National Archives (TNA) has statutory responsibilities under the Public Records Act (PRA) to provide guidance and supervision to public bodies on the safekeeping, management, and selection of public records. This includes issuing guidance, training, and developing tools that help organisations manage information effectively and ensure that records of long-term historical value can be identified and transferred to TNA for permanent preservation.

To support this activity, TNA is developing Manage Records Compliance (MRC), a new digital solution designed to provide a single access point for oversight of public records compliance across government. The vision for MRC is to bring together key reporting and assessment functions into one streamlined platform, enabling more consistent, efficient and transparent oversight of how public bodies manage their information.

The MRC Data Analyst will play a crucial role in transforming the information collected through this new user-focused digital service into clear, reliable business intelligence. This includes developing reporting pipelines, dashboards and analytical outputs that will be available to both TNA and public bodies, enabling them to understand compliance levels, monitor progress, identify trends and risks, and make evidence-based decisions.

This role is central to TNA’s mission to systematically monitor compliance across government and enhance the quality of its support and services to government and the wider public sector. It is a 12 month fixed term position, with the possibility of extension.

Application Process


Interview: Interviews will be held on-site. As part of the interview you will be required to present a dashboard and an underlying data model you have developed
Personal Statement: Please submit work history details and a personal statement, not exceeding 1200 words. Selection for interview will be based on the 6 essential criteria shown below so please ensure that your application demonstrates in detail how you meet these requirements. You may wish to use the STAR method to help you write your personal statement. You can find out more about the STAR method here: https://nationalcareers.service.gov.uk/careers-advice/interview-advice/the-star-method


Essential Criteria


Academic and/or professional qualification in business analytics, data analysis, data science or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.
Strong experience in data modelling, transformation, and validation.
Proficiency with data visualisation tools (including Power BI) and associated languages (e.g. DAX).
Ability to design maintainable reporting pipelines and combine multiple datasets to produce reliable insights.
Strong analytical capability, including the ability to identify patterns, trends, and risks within complex data.
Experience producing analytical outputs aligned to user needs and business requirements.


In the event of a high volume of applications we will conduct an initial sift based on the first criteria.

SC clearance/willingness to obtain SC clearance will be required for this role. This requires candidates to have been resident in the UK for at least the past three years. Please do not apply if you have been resident in the UK for less than three years as your application will be rejected.

ALL of the essential criteria listed in the job description below will be assessed at interview.

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Job Description

Role and Responsibilities

Build and maintain analytical data pipelines


Develop robust processes for cleaning, transforming and modelling data submitted by public bodies.
Ensure data is accurate, consistent and ready for reporting and analysis.
Work with colleagues responsible for application development and form creation, to ensure data is captured appropriately.


Develop dashboards and business intelligence products


Design high quality dashboards and visualisations to support compliance monitoring and sector-level insight.
Produce reports for TNA teams and accessible outputs for public bodies using the service.
Work with user researchers and other stakeholders, to ensure dashboards and other data products are accurate, high quality, and easy to understand.
Support the development of self-service analytical tools within MRC.


Lead on data quality and consistency


Establish and maintain good practices and processes for data validation, documentation and version control.
Work collaboratively across the service to improve data structures and metadata quality.


Insight generation and decision support


Identify trends, risks, patterns and opportunities from the collected data.
Produce insights that strengthen TNA’s services, advice and engagement with public bodies.
Prepare analysis for senior leadership and contribute to internal strategic discussions.


Collaboration and stakeholder engagement


Engage with internal teams, delivery partners and public bodies to understand analytical needs.
Contribute to user testing of reporting functions and dashboards.
Work effectively within an agile, multidisciplinary environment.


Person Specification

Essential Criteria


Academic and/or professional qualification in business analytics, data analysis, data science or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.
Strong experience in data modelling, transformation, and validation.
Proficiency with data visualisation tools (including Power BI) and associated languages (e.g. DAX).
Ability to design maintainable reporting pipelines and combine multiple datasets to produce reliable insights.
Strong analytical capability, including the ability to identify patterns, trends, and risks within complex data.
Experience producing analytical outputs aligned to user needs and business requirements.
Ability to work in agile or iterative delivery environments and collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present complex analytical findings clearly to non‑technical audiences, alongside good organisational skills to manage concurrent work.


Desirable criteria


Experience developing dashboards or reporting to support assurance, oversight or performance monitoring.
Interest in information management, public records or data‑driven public sector services.
Awareness of how data can be prepared for, or integrated into, AI or advanced analytics pipelines.


Other Requirements


A combination of onsite and home working is currently available. Applicants must be able to work from our Kew site at least 60% of their time, possibly more when new to the role.
SC clearance is required for this role. This requires candidates to have been resident in the UK for at least the past three years.


The Civil Service is committed to attract, retain and invest in talent wherever it is

found. To learn more please see the Civil Service People Plan and the Civil Service

D&I Strategy.

Benefits

Generous benefits package, including pension, sports and social club facilities, onsite gym, discounted rates at our on-site cafe and opportunities for training and development. Annual leave entitlement of 22 days per calendar year (rising to 25 after the first year, and incrementally to 30 days after six years) and 10½ days public and privilege holidays per annum.

Any move to The National Archives from another employer will mean you can no longer access childcare vouchers. This includes moves between government departments. You may however be eligible for other government schemes, including Tax-Free Childcare. Determine your eligibility at  https://www.childcarechoices.gov.uk/ (opens in new window)

Reasonable adjustments

If a person with disabilities is put at a substantial disadvantage compared to a non-disabled person, we have a duty to make reasonable changes to our processes.

If you need a change to be made so that you can make your application, you should:


Contact The National Archives via careers@nationalarchives.gov.uk as soon as possible before the closing date to discuss your needs
Complete the ‘Reasonable Adjustments’ section of your application form to tell us what changes or help you might need further on in the recruitment process. For instance, you may need wheelchair access at interview, or if you’re deaf, a Language Service Professional


Feedback will only be provided if you attend an interview or assessment.

Security

Successful candidates must pass a disclosure and barring security check.

People working with government assets must complete basic personnel security standard checks (opens in new window)

Nationality Requirements

This job is broadly open to the following groups:


UK nationals
nationals of the Republic of Ireland
nationals of Commonwealth countries who have the right to work in the UK
nationals of the EU, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland or Liechtenstein and family members of those nationalities with settled or pre-settled status under the European Union Settlement Scheme (EUSS) (opens in a new window)
nationals of the EU, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland or Liechtenstein and family members of those nationalities who have made a valid application for settled or pre-settled status under the European Union Settlement Scheme (EUSS)
individuals with limited leave to remain or indefinite leave to remain who were eligible to apply for EUSS on or before 31 December 2020
Turkish nationals, and certain family members of Turkish nationals, who have accrued the right to work in the Civil Service


Further information on nationality requirements (opens in new window)

Working for the Civil Service

The Civil Service Code  (opens in new window) sets out the standards of behaviour expected of civil servants.

We recruit by merit on the basis of fair and open competition, as outlined in the Civil Service Commission's recruitment principles. (opens in new window)

The Civil Service embraces diversity and promotes equal opportunities. As such, we run a Disability Confident Scheme (DCS) for candidates with disabilities who meet the minimum selection criteria.

The Civil Service also offers a Redeployment Interview Scheme to civil servants who are at risk of redundancy, and who meet the minimum requirements for the advertised vacancy.

This vacancy is part of the Great Place to Work for Veterans initiative. (opens in new window)

Contact Point For Applicants


Name: The National Archives Recruitment Team
Email: careers@nationalarchives.gov.uk


Further information

If you feel your application has not been treated in accordance with the Recruitment Principles and you wish to make a complaint, in the first instance, you should contact The National Archives via email: careers@nationalarchives.gov.uk If you are not satisfied with the response you receive from the Department, you can contact the Civil Service Commission at https://civilservicecommission.independent.gov.uk/recruitment/recruitment-complaints/ (opens in new window)

Title: Accounts Assistant
Description:
Company Overview

You will be joining a modern Law Firm as their Accounts Assistant on a full-time, permanent basis. Working in a close-knit team of 4 consisting of a Billing Clerk and a Legal Cashier, you will all be reporting directly into the Practice Manager. This role will be based 5 days a week in their luxurious Central London offices. There will be flexibility to work from home 1 day a week after 3 months with the business.




Role Responsibilities

You will be responsible for assisting with raising client invoices in a timely and accurate manner, allocating fee-earner time and costs, maintaining the firm's Billing document, assisting with mid-year and year-end financial processes, supporting with reconciliations and reporting, assisting with office expenses and drafting requests for funds on account. You will will post disbursements and recharges, contribute to continuous improvement of financial processes and documentation and provide ad-hoc support to the wider team as required.




Role Requirements

To be successful in this role, you will need to have previous experience in an Accounts or Finance support role, have a good knowledge of Excel, have strong attention to detail and accuracy, possess excellent time management skills and be a clear, confident communicator, with the ability to collaborate with colleagues at all levels.




In Return

You will receive an annual salary of up to £32,000 (depending on experience) + Study Support + Benefits and this package will be regularly reviewed to remain in line with market rate. You will be working for a fast-growing, modern and very prestigious Law Firm that can provide fantastic career progression opportunities in the future. You will be part of a close-knit, supportive and high-performing team who will be there to assist with your transition into the business from day one. You will be located in a great area of London, working in a new and luxurious office, close to multiple transport links and top attractions. Once you have been with the firm for 3 months, you will have the option to work from home for 1 day a week.




What to do now

If you would like to apply for this role, please do so via this advert or email me directly at sian.prest@weareinvestigo.com

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are BLG: Canada’s Law Firm. Our culture is one of vigilance and curiosity, Firm-wide collaboration, unfailing mutual respect and dedication. We pride ourselves on having standout talent and delivering an exceptional client experience.







We are a future-minded Firm delivering high-value advice and known for our unwavering commitment to innovation, diversity and inclusion, community involvement and talent development. Learn more about us at www.blg.com.




Purpose of Role




This is a full-time, 6 month contract opportunity




The Analyst, Data Management will report to the Senior Manager, Data Management and will contribute to the development and execution of plans, policies, and practices that control, protect, deliver and enhance the value of BLG’s data and information assets.







This role will be responsible for assisting in the implementation of enterprise data strategy, initiation and successful execution of strategic data management projects and continuous improvement initiatives.




Key Responsibilities




The Analyst, Data Management will be responsible for maintaining data definitions, establishing data quality standards, monitoring compliance to those standards, improving data quality, assist in the development of master/reference data and business intelligence/analytical solutions.




The role will contribute to the implementation of data warehouse platform using Microsoft Fabric, with a focus on centralization and the protection of sensitive data through creation of appropriate organization policies, standards, principles, governance metrics, processes, related tools and data architecture.




The incumbent will be required to work with diverse stakeholders/data owners to understand current and future data needs and work with Senior Manager, Data Management to assist with the development of roadmaps (tactical and strategic).




Assist with implementing a data management and governance framework aligned with BLG’s business objectives.
Assist with the implementation of data catalog solution, to hold firm-wide technical metadata and data glossary
Assist with the historical data quality/cleanup initiatives and develop rules to validate the data across different enterprise systems.
Work with sponsors and stakeholders to implement processes, policies, firm-wide business data definitions, data quality rules, domains, validation and resolution policies and method that will support a successful data management program.
Implement master/reference data management standards, enforce data governance procedures and ensure data integrity across multiple platforms to ensure proper use of data.
Establish monitoring and reporting processes to ensure high standards of data quality and conformance with data policies, procedures and standards.
Develop indicators of performance, quality metrics and ensure compliance with data related policies, standards, roles and responsibilities.
Develop & maintain inventory of the enterprise data sources, including authoritative systems and owners.
Provide recommendations on the development of data management policies, procedures and standards to ensure best practices of data management. Assist with the formulation of new guidance and standards.
Analyze business processes and identify data and information requirements (i.e. what information the business process requires, produces, collects and stores; who needs access to it; for what purpose and for how long). Recommend improvements in business processes as appropriate.
Ensure compliance with roles and responsibilities related to data governance and ensure clear data ownership and stewardship of the BLGs data assets.
Participate in Data Governance Council meetings, comprising of Data Owners, communicate decisions, plans, goals and strategies related to enterprise data.
Coordinate with data stewards and data owners across the enterprise to address data quality issues.
Collaborate with centralized analytics team to deliver business intelligence solutions
Maintain best practice guidelines and standards for data, metadata, data modelling, and management.
Investigate tools and technologies to assist with data management and dissemination, such as data warehouse and analytics tools, data integration tools, data quality and profiling tools, outlining risks, costs, benefits, and capabilities.
Responsible for maintaining and enhancing the data quality scorecards




Key Competencies

Experience implementing best practices, adoption methodologies and standards with regards to Data Analytics, Data Governance, Data Structure, Data Architecture, and Data Security.
Knowledgeable in data profiling, root cause analysis, and quality improvement.
Knowledge of Microsoft Fabric, Python notebooks, Data Pipeline, Data Factory is an asset.
Knowledge of DAMA framework is an asset.
Well versed in the following data domains: Master/Reference Data, Operational Data, Analytical Data, Unstructured Data, and Metadata.
Solid understanding of transactional and information systems, data warehouses, data marts, MDM methodologies and life cycles.
Hands-on experience with data analysis, quality, cleaning and extraction / validation tools.
Knowledge of Reporting tools like SSRS, Power BI is an asset.
Strong proficiency with SQL-based languages. Ability to write and troubleshoot complex SQL queries.
Hands-on experience with implementation of a data catalog/dictionary.
Minimum 2 years of experience in Data Management design principles, concepts and implementing data-centric solutions.
Minimum 2 years of experience with BI methodology and technologies.
Knowledge of Informatica suite of tools is an asset.
Ability to translate user requirements into technical specifications and systems.
Understanding the IT architecture discipline, processes, concepts, and best practices.




The expected salary range for this position is $90,000 - $110,000, depending on level of experience, region/location, and other factors.

BLG is committed to building a diverse workplace reflective of the communities we serve and to fostering an inclusive culture where Firm members feel valued, respected and inspired to thrive as their authentic selves. We value diversity of thought and the unique skills, perspectives and experience each individual brings to BLG. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates and encourage applicants from members of groups that have been historically underrepresented, including but not limited to First Nations, Métis and Inuit Peoples, racialized individuals, persons with disabilities, people who identify as women and/or LGBTQ2S+.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About The Team

Data Science at Stripe is a vibrant community where data analysts and data scientists learn and grow together. You will work with some of Stripe’s most fundamental and exciting data, and use that data to help drive company-wide initiatives. We have a variety of Data Analytics roles and teams across Stripe and will seek to align you to the most relevant team based on your background.

What you'll do

In this role, you will partner deeply with teams across Stripe to ensure that our users, our products, and our business have the models, data products, and insights needed to make decisions and grow responsibly. You will work closely with partners to extract insights from Stripe's rich and complex data. You will also work with leaders to translate business needs into data problems. You will build metrics, scalable data pipelines, dashboards, and reports to inform and run the business. You will deliver actionable business recommendations through analyses and data storytelling.

Who you are

We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.

Minimum Requirements


MS/MA + 2 years or BS/BA + 3 years of full time experience exclusive of internships in Business Intelligence Engineering, Data Analyst, Business Analyst roles.
Proficiency in SQL.
Proven ability to manage and deliver on multiple projects with great attention to detail
Ability to clearly communicate results and drive impact.
Ability to design, implement, and maintain data pipelines and dashboards to generate actionable insights based on stakeholder requirements.
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver strategic insights, benchmarks, and analyses that provide recommendations.
Ability to enable stakeholders and partners by building self-service tooling and providing training to empower stakeholder teams to be data literate and self-sufficient in autonomous reporting capabilities.



Preferred Qualifications


Prior experience at a growth stage internet or software company.
Experience with distributed data frameworks like Spark to write and debug data pipelines.
Good understanding of development processes and best practices like engineering standards, code reviews, and testing.
Strong statistical knowledge.
Working knowledge of Python.
Experience creating leadership level reporting, such as QBRs, MBRs.



In-office expectations

Office-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible.

Pay and benefits

The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is CA$121,100 - CA$221,900. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process.

Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

Title: Data Analyst - Fintech (m/f/d)
Description:
We are TRADER, a Canadian leader in digital automotive solutions. Our flagship brands - AutoTrader.ca, AutoSync, Dealertrack Canada and CMS - help Canadians buy, sell, and finance vehicles with confidence.

Learn more at tradercorporation.com.

As part of AutoScout24 group, Europe’s largest online car marketplace, we’re shaping the future of automotive retail in Canada and beyond.

As Data Analyst, you will support data-driven decision-making across our Fintech products including Dealertrack and CMS. You will work closely with product and business teams to analyze transaction flows, fraud performance, and customer journeys to generate actionable insights. Your work will help improve product strategy, strengthen fraud prevention, and increase customer trust while supporting the modernization of core Fintech platforms.

What you’ll do:


Partner with Fintech product teams to analyze Dealertrack and CMS data and support product decisions
Conduct deep-dive analyses on product funnels, transaction flows and customer journeys
Measure and monitor key fintech KPIs using established analytical data models
Translate business questions into structured analyses and clear recommendations
Analyze fraud prevention performance and identify risks and improvement opportunities
Communicate insights to technical and non-technical stakeholders to support decision-making


What you’ll need


3+ years of experience in data analytics in product, fintech, saas or platform environments
Strong SQL skills and experience with bi tools such as power BI, Tableau or QuickSight
Experience using GenAI tools to improve productivity in SQL development, data exploration, and documentation
Experience working with financial, transactional or operational datasets
Ability to work closely with product and business stakeholders to deliver insights
Strong communication skills and ability to present analytical findings clearly
Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset with focus on data quality and accuracy


What’s in it for you

We understand that there is life at work and life outside of work. Here are a few benefits that support us to be our creative best.


Gym discounts
Employee and Family Assistance program
Virtual wellness events
Conferences & training budget
Regular internal training programs
Financial planning with 3% matching Pension
Competitive salary
Annual bonus structure


The base salary range for this position is CAD $90.000 – $120.000.

This range reflects the expected compensation at the time of posting. The final offer may vary and can be higher based on relevant skills, experience, location, and market conditions. Based on the role, the total rewards package may also include benefits, bonus, and other employee offerings.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Who We Are




Teranet is Canada’s leader in the delivery and transformation of statutory registry services with extensive expertise in land and commercial registries. We also market insightful property and data solutions, as well as practice management automation to thousands of customers in real estate, financial services, government, utilities, and legal markets.




Connect. Grow. Thrive Together.




To learn more about who we are visit our website: www.teranet.ca




About the Role




The Data & Analytics team within IT line of business has an ambitious mandate, where we are accountable for leveraging Data & Analytics to deliver business value both internally as well as to external clients through the following:

Enhance existing or create new products and services with proprietary data assets and partnering with external data providers, through the application of data science
Design and deliver cutting-edge reporting solutions to bring data to life
Conduct advanced data analysis to generate business insights that drive strategic decision-making
Demonstrate thought leadership in the ecosystem in which we operate
Provide actionable recommendations to improve overall organizational efficiencies




In addition, this team will play an important role in evangelizing and educating the value of Data & Analytics to the rest of the organization, in support of cultivating a data-driven culture.

The Data Analyst will support these ambitious goals primarily through ensuring seamless data integration, maintaining high quality data standards, collaborating with cross functional teams, and delivering comprehensive analyses and innovative visualization using business intelligence tools.




What You’ll Be Doing




The Data Analyst supports the team in the design, development, and implementation of data & analytics use cases through the following responsibilities:




Data research and collection (40%)

Engage with stakeholders to understand business requirements and objectives for data & analytics use cases
Research, identify and then collect / extract the external data sets required for the use case. Creativity, thinking outside of the box, and resourcefulness is required to find new and relevant data sources to meet business requirements.
Explore data to build foundational data knowledge in subject domain
Engage with internal data governance and legal teams to ensure data license terms and conditions are aligned with Teranet standards
Develop an inventory to actively keep track of statuses of various data assets (eg. Procured, investigated but not procured, to be investigated)
Continuously monitor data landscape to review, update, and curate data sources and assets




Data Exploration (20%)

Collaborate across marketing, product, finance, partner solutions, data engineering and other teams to deeply understand business objectives
Translate business questions into technical data needs, ensuring alignment with project goals
Gather internal and external datasets creatively, ensuring relevance to business use cases
Exhibit strong attention to detail while working with large datasets to ensure accuracy, completeness and high-quality standards
Present insights clearly through reports and presentations tailored to diverse audiences




Data Analytics and Visualization (40%)

Create interactive dashboards and reports that guide business decisions
Demonstrate curiosity by exploring datasets, conducting exploratory data analysis to uncover hidden trends, outliers, anomalies, and identify insights that might not be obvious
Provide detailed analysis of customer behavior, market trends, and competitive landscape to support product, sales and marketing strategies
Use visualization techniques to communicate insights effectively and intuitively







About You




You are someone who:

Is keen to understand the business – data analytics cannot be done in a vacuum – we need to be close to the business so that we understand what we are trying to solve for and what the data means
Tells stories from data – data analytics is not data dump – it needs to be meaningful and intuitive to the end user in order to realize the inherent business value
Challenges the status quo – the state of business and the field of data analytics are constantly evolving, and we need to as well. Assumptions and old ways of doing things are open season, as long as you bring along workable and relevant solutions articulated in a professional manner. Proactively pursue and learn about new data sets, analytical tools and methodologies to solve problems in new and innovative ways.
Is good at problem solving – someone who can “connect the dots”, and have a logical and methodical way to tackle new and complex problems
Takes pride in your work – your work is diligent and thorough, and you are proud of your reputation for high-quality and reliable work
Is a quick study – the successful candidate must be curious, resourceful, and dig into the weeds to ask the right questions and figure things out independently
Is a good communicator – someone who is polished and can communicate effectively with internal and external parties to ensure alignment on data analysis and reporting needs




Technical qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in quantitative field: Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering
Minimum 2 years of work experience as a Data Analyst
Proficiency in SQL, Python and Excel
Advanced expertise in Tableau, or other BI tools
Knowledge of Big Data Technologies (HDFS, Spark, etc.) and Cloud platforms (AWS and/or Azure)
Understanding of spatial data concepts, including geospatial data formats (e.g., GeoJSON, Shapefiles), spatial analysis, and tools like ArcGIS




Let’s Talk Pay




We believe in being upfront about pay and helping you make informed decisions about your career. The annual pay range for this role is $70,000 - $90,000 CAD, inclusive of base salary and target incentive pay. We understand that great talent comes in many forms, each with unique skills, experience, and potential. Your salary will be tailored to reflect the experience you bring and the impact you’re ready to have on this role.




At Teranet, we also know that compensation extends far beyond a pay cheque. Along with your cash compensation, we offer a comprehensive package which includes the following:




100% Employer Paid Health Benefit Plan
Employer Matching Retirement Savings Plan
Paid Vacation, Floater Days & Sick Leaves
Maternity, Parental and/or Adoption Leave Top-Up Programs
Corporate Discounts & GoodLife Group Rate Membership
Employee Assistance Program – for you and your loved ones!




Our Human Approach to Hiring




At Teranet, we use smart technology to make hiring faster and more efficient. Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools help us review applications and identify strong matches—but the real decisions? Those are made by our awesome people.




Every interaction you’ll have, and every decision we make, is led by humans. AI may support us, but it’s human connection that drives every hire.




At Teranet, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we live and serve. If you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.




Come As You Are. We Like You That Way!

  • Demonstrate curiosity by exploring datasets, conducting exploratory data analysis to uncover hidden trends, outliers, anomalies, and identify insights that might not be obvious
  • Provide detailed analysis of customer behavior, market trends, and competitive landscape to support product, sales and marketing strategies
  • Use visualization techniques to communicate insights effectively and intuitively



About You


You are someone who:

  • Is keen to understand the business – data analytics cannot be done in a vacuum – we need to be close to the business so that we understand what we are trying to solve for and what the data means
  • Tells stories from data – data analytics is not data dump – it needs to be meaningful and intuitive to the end user in order to realize the inherent business value
  • Challenges the status quo – the state of business and the field of data analytics are constantly evolving, and we need to as well. Assumptions and old ways of doing things are open season, as long as you bring along workable and relevant solutions articulated in a professional manner. Proactively pursue and learn about new data sets, analytical tools and methodologies to solve problems in new and innovative ways.
  • Is good at problem solving – someone who can “connect the dots”, and have a logical and methodical way to tackle new and complex problems
  • Takes pride in your work – your work is diligent and thorough, and you are proud of your reputation for high-quality and reliable work
  • Is a quick study – the successful candidate must be curious, resourceful, and dig into the weeds to ask the right questions and figure things out independently
  • Is a good communicator – someone who is polished and can communicate effectively with internal and external parties to ensure alignment on data analysis and reporting needs


Technical qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in quantitative field: Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering
  • Minimum 2 years of work experience as a Data Analyst
  • Proficiency in SQL, Python and Excel
  • Advanced expertise in Tableau, or other BI tools
  • Knowledge of Big Data Technologies (HDFS, Spark, etc.) and Cloud platforms (AWS and/or Azure)
  • Understanding of spatial data concepts, including geospatial data formats (e.g., GeoJSON, Shapefiles), spatial analysis, and tools like ArcGIS


Let’s Talk Pay


We believe in being upfront about pay and helping you make informed decisions about your career. The annual pay range for this role is $70,000 - $90,000 CAD, inclusive of base salary and target incentive pay. We understand that great talent comes in many forms, each with unique skills, experience, and potential. Your salary will be tailored to reflect the experience you bring and the impact you’re ready to have on this role.


At Teranet, we also know that compensation extends far beyond a pay cheque. Along with your cash compensation, we offer a comprehensive package which includes the following:


  • 100% Employer Paid Health Benefit Plan
  • Employer Matching Retirement Savings Plan
  • Paid Vacation, Floater Days & Sick Leaves
  • Maternity, Parental and/or Adoption Leave Top-Up Programs
  • Corporate Discounts & GoodLife Group Rate Membership
  • Employee Assistance Program – for you and your loved ones!


Our Human Approach to Hiring


At Teranet, we use smart technology to make hiring faster and more efficient. Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools help us review applications and identify strong matches—but the real decisions? Those are made by our awesome people.


Every interaction you’ll have, and every decision we make, is led by humans. AI may support us, but it’s human connection that drives every hire.


At Teranet, we are committed to fostering an inclusive, accessible environment, where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. We are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our customers and the communities in which we live and serve. If you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.


Come As You Are. We Like You That Way!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Here at Rakuten Kobo Inc. we offer a casual working start-up environment and a group of friendly and talented individuals. Our employees rank us highly in terms of commitment to work/life balance. We realize that for our people to be innovative, creative and passionate they need to feel valued and supported. We believe in rewarding all our employees with competitive salaries, performance based annual bonuses, stock options and training opportunities.

If you’re looking for a company that inspires passion, personal, and professional growth – join Kobo and come help us on our mission of making reading lives better.

The Role

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data Storytelling & Insights team, working closely with our engineering foundations. This role blends strong technical ownership of analytics‑ready data with the ability to translate complex information into clear, actionable insights. You’ll work end‑to‑end across the analytics lifecycle—building scalable, trusted data models and partnering with stakeholders to deliver dashboards and analyses that inform key business decisions.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain analytics‑ready data models and pipelines using Dataform and BigQuery, including migrating and modernizing legacy logic into clearer, more reusable, and better governed data assets.
Ensure analytics code and pipelines are reliable, well‑documented, and suited for downstream reporting, experimentation, and analysis.
Build and maintain Tableau dashboards and reports that are reusable, trustworthy, and grounded in centralized, well‑modeled data.
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear analyses that explain what happened, why it happened, and what to do next.
Partner with business stakeholders and analytics peers to define requirements, metrics, and scalable analytics solutions.
Contribute to shared analytics standards and best practices across SQL, Dataform, Tableau, and documentation.
Leverage modern AI tools and LLM‑based coding agents to accelerate analytics workflows (e.g., SQL development, modeling, debugging, documentation), while applying strong judgment to validate correctness, performance, and data quality.


The Skill Set

Must-haves


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or related field.
5+ years of experience in data analytics, analytics engineering, or BI, with demonstrated ownership of analytics data pipelines and models.
Strong SQL skills, including complex joins, window functions, and working with semi‑structured or nested data.
Solid understanding of data modeling and designing tables for downstream analytics and reporting use cases.
Strong analytical thinking, problem‑solving, and communication skills.
Experience working with modern cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred).
Hands‑on experience with Dataform (experience with dbt is a plus).
Experience with BI and visualization tools such as Tableau, Looker, or Domo.
Familiarity with analytics engineering best practices (e.g., testing, CI/CD for data).


Nice to haves


Experience using LLM‑based coding assistants to support analytics or data engineering workflows.
Working knowledge of Python for analysis, automation, or ML‑adjacent use cases.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (Google Cloud Platform preferred).
Experience with Git‑based version control and analytics workflow automation.


The Perks


Flexible hours and hybrid remote working environment
Subsidized in-office lunches
Four extended long weekends in the summer
Full benefits starting from your first day
Paid Volunteer days, unlimited sick days, and 3% RRSP matching
Work From Anywhere Program for up to 3 months (90 days)
Lifestyle spending account
Talent and development training budget
Free Kobo device + free weekly e-book or audiobook
Weekly Kobo Tech University sessions
Maternity/paternity leave top up
Dog friendly office


About Rakuten Kobo Inc.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is the world’s digital bookseller created by and for booklovers. Owned by Tokyo-based Rakuten Group, Inc. and headquartered in Toronto, Rakuten Kobo’s millions of worldwide users can read anytime, anywhere, and on any device. With a mission to make reading lives better for all, Rakuten Kobo connects readers to stories using thoughtful and personalized curation of eBooks and audiobooks, and the best dedicated eReaders and apps for reading. With the singular focus of making reading lives the best they can be, Kobo’s open platform allows people to fit reading into more moments in their busy lives. To learn more about Rakuten Kobo, visit www.kobo.com.

Rakuten Kobo Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Accessibility accommodations for candidates with disabilities participating in the selection process are available on request. Any information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially.  

Rakuten Kobo would like to thank all applicants for their interest in this role however only qualified candidates will be shortlisted.  

#RKIND

Five Principles for Success

Our worldwide practices describe specific behaviors that make Rakuten unique and united across the world. We expect Rakuten employees to model these 5 Shugi Principles of Success.

Always improve, Always Advance - Only be satisfied with complete success - Kaizen

Passionately Professional - Take an uncompromising approach to your work and be determined to be the best

Hypothesize - Practice - Validate – Shikumika - Use the Rakuten Cycle to succeed in unknown territory

Maximize Customer Satisfaction - The greatest satisfaction for our teams is seeing their customers smile

Speed!! Speed!! Speed!! - Always be conscious of time - take charge, set clear goals, and engage your team

The successful applicant for this role will be eligible for stock options, health, vision, dental insurance, RRSP matching, Personal Time Off (PTO), Volunteer Time Off (VTO), and other employee benefits as the company implements.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Scope:

The Data and Content Management Division uses a one-government approach to information and privacy governance, decision-making, and service delivery across the Government of Alberta (GoA) balanced with individual client needs. This facilitates enhanced data access, collaboration, reduction in data duplication, and innovation to ensure effective and efficient services across the government to provide better services to Albertans.

The Data Centre of Excellence requires a resource to support the design and development of analytical data products & services to enable the GoA to better leverage its data assets support implementation of the GoA Data Strategy. Working within a team, this resource is an expert, and plays a key role in ensuring high-quality data modeling, applying algorithms, de-identification, synthetic data creation, visualizations and development of evidence for use across ministries and with external stakeholders. This role will enact the pillars of the Data Strategy by using data to benefit Albertans

The successful candidate will be a Data Analytics Specialist / Data Scientist with a diverse range of analytical skills and experience in working in multi-faceted roles. The role may have aspects of any or all the following: data analysis, data science, data modelling, statistical analysis, artificial intelligence (AI) & machine learning (ML), strategizing, advising, data product design and delivery. There may be aspects of data engineering, technical analysis, and business analysis.

Duties:

Provides hands-on support, leadership, advice and direction on the strategic data initiatives that are being undertaken as part of the Data Strategy. A critical responsibility is to coordinate with various internal and external clients to understand their analytics needs and how to use the data to best meet these needs. This resource is an expert in anticipating, identifying and responding to diverse and complex data analytic requirements across GoA departments and from external organizations, while also aligning with other areas of branch and broader department. Services and project deliverables should evolve as the work progresses, in response to emerging user and business needs, as well as design and technical opportunities. Works with Manager Analytics Capability Centre to:

• Provide expertise and leadership in the design and competition of analytic projects

• Develop and share data models and products

• Provide continuous improvement of analytics capacities

• Design and support development of analytic service offerings

• Analyze and organize raw data, preparing it for prescriptive and predictive modeling, while building algorithms that deliver business value.

• Evaluating business needs, enhancing data quality, and designing analytical tools to support our data services.

• Conducting complex data analysis and collaborating with data engineers and analysts on various projects.

• Coaching and mentorship team members, fostering a client-centric approach and encouraging innovative solutions.

• Working closely with senior management to support a cultural shift toward data as a strategic asset and maintain effective relationships with internal and external stakeholders.

• Create and present options, roadmaps, frameworks, models, and briefings for senior executives with regards to analytics services, based on best and emerging practices and principles.

• Facilitate strategic conversations to develop shared understanding and generate options for decision-making to align diverse stakeholder interests and goals.

• Develop baseline and ongoing outcomes, key results, metrics, and other indicators.

• Work as part of a team responsible for the generalizable extraction of knowledge

• from data by applying various techniques and methodologies including probability models, machine learning, computer programming, statistics, data engineering, pattern recognition and learning, and data visualizations

• Experience in Data Analytics or as a Data Scientist.

• Apply skills to provide insights, support decision-making and facilitate strategic business planning across the department.

• Requires a focus on understanding predictive analytics and needs-based analytics strategies to simplify, consistently produce and re-use analytical models and assets to drive measurable value.

• Provide executives and decision makers a deeper understanding of their operations,

• transactions, services and information required for them to identify new opportunities

• that can only be uncovered through analytics.

• Provide depth and insights on GoA’s data assets and transform them into meaningful analytics for decision making

• Bring knowledge of statistical classification techniques such as k-means and hierarchical clustering, partition trees, and logistic regression.

• Integrate both quantitative and qualitative data to create business insights.

• Design and create dashboards and custom reporting with various data sources and inputs.

• Analyze data and prepare results.

• Gather and document client requirements.

• Capture business and technical metadata for analytical products.

• Escalate issues and risks, as appropriate.

• Work within a multi-vendor/staff environment

  • Leverage Azure services such as ADF, ADLS, Synapse for data discovery and validation
  • Support end-to-end analytics lifecycle including data transformation logic and reporting enablement
  • Ensure data quality, governance, and alignment with enterprise data architecture and business outcomes

Title: Associate Data Analyst
Description:
Collaborating with the Director, Data Design and GWAM Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) Business Support team, the Associate Data Analyst, will be a key member of a team of data professionals responsible for delivering key critical initiatives and establishing the data quality program for Manulife GWAM. You will couple your data analysis experience with a solid understanding of the Investment Segment’s business and operations to implement the data strategy.

Position Responsibilities


You will work with Global Wealth Asset Management Operational Business & Technical Support teams, GWAM Business Teams and the GWAM Chief Data Office to support the Enterprise Data Lake (EDL) daily production activities to ensure data is available, reliable, consistent, accessible, secure, and timely through daily data issue identification, analysis and action planning.
You will partner with the GWAM Chief Data Office Data Governance and Data Analytics, support on-going EDL process improvements, including enhancements to GWAM operational capabilities, GWAM data quality monitoring and controls and GWAM meta data management tools such as the business glossary and data lineage.
Working with different GWAM teams to support EDL design and development for GWAM strategic initiatives.


What We Are Looking For


Highly analytical with strong consulting and problem-solving abilities; tenacity to identify the root cause of problems and apply innovative approaches to solve business data problems
Strong service orientation
Take personal accountability for issue analysis and resolution
Asset management or financial services industry experience
Previous experience in the management of large, firm-wide processes
Effective communication skills
Constructive challenge and influencing skills
Experience in translating between business and technology worlds
Champion of change and effectively managing the implementation of new ideas / improvements
Confident when dealing with investment professionals, senior management and other partners
Knowledge and experience with asset management systems and platforms such as trading/order management, portfolio accounting, client reporting, performance analytics and master data management
Technical Skills, such as knowledge of SQL required to perform data analysis
Familiarity with Microsoft Power Platform tools is considered an asset


Qualifications


1-5 years of relevant experience
SQL (data analysis): 1 year’s hands-on experience
Bachelor’s or Master’s in fields like Finance, Financial Engineering, Accounting, or Business (with concentrations in Investments, Analytics, or Information Systems).
Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Automate, Power Apps): 1–2 years
Asset management systems/platforms (e.g., OMS/trading, portfolio accounting, client reporting, performance analytics, MDM): 2–5 years
Large, firm‑wide process management: 2–3 years


When You Join Our Team


We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our global team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview

BET99 is Canada's Premiere Online Sportsbook and Casino. Launched in 2020, we have consistently innovated the online gaming landscape every step of the way, exponentially growing our customer base and, in turn, our internal BET99 team! We’re a fast-paced company and are looking for individuals who live and breathe our core values: challenging the status quo, trust, transparency, integrity, and collaboration.




As a Data Analyst, you will play a key role in helping teams across the business make informed decisions by translating data into clear, actionable insights. You bring strong analytical skills, curiosity to explore business problems, and the ability to communicate findings effectively to non-technical stakeholders.




In our fast-paced online sportsbook and casino environment, you’ll work closely with stakeholders in product, marketing, and operations to analyse performance, surface insights, and support data-driven decision-making. You’ll contribute to building reliable, scalable analytics and reporting while continuously developing your technical and analytical capabilities




Vacancy Status: New




Responsibilities

Partner with stakeholders to understand business questions and translate them into analytical tasks and data requirements
Analyse datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and insights related to player behaviour, marketing performance, and operational KPIs
Write clear, efficient SQL queries to extract and structure data from Snowflake and other sources
Use Python where appropriate to clean, transform, and analyse data
Build and maintain dashboards and standard reports in Power BI to support ongoing business visibility
Deliver campaign and product analysis, including segmentation, funnel analysis, and performance tracking
Contribute to defining, documenting, and monitoring core business metrics
Communicate insights through clear narratives, visualisations, and written summaries tailored to different audiences
Own analytical tasks end-to-end with minimal supervision, escalating ambiguity or data quality issues when appropriate
Collaborate with data engineering and analytics teammates to ensure data quality, consistency, and scalability of outputs




Requirements

2–5 years of experience in a data analyst or business intelligence role
Strong SQL skills and experience working with analytical databases (Snowflake experience is a plus)
Hands-on experience with BI tools, ideally Power BI
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis
Experience analysing product, customer, or operational data (experience in iGaming or digital products is a plus but not required)
Solid understanding of metrics, KPIs, and basic statistical concepts
Ability to communicate insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
A pragmatic, detail-oriented mindset with a desire to continuously improve analytics and reporting




Nice to Have

Experience with experimentation or A/B test analysis
Exposure to dbt or analytics engineering concepts




Why Join BET99?

Come for the career… stay for the culture.

Canada-wide remote freedom - your office is anywhere you want it to be.
Time off that feels good - paid vacation, paid sick days, plus your birthday off on us.
Early access to leadership - meet the CEO in your first 30 days and see how your work truly makes an impact.
Open-door leadership style - transparency and collaboration are core to how we operate.
Premium benefits - 100% employer paid, for you and your family.
Built-in career acceleration - Mentorship Programs led by industry veterans.
A community you’ll want to be part of - book clubs, monthly employee events, cross-team fun, and more.
Bring a friend, earn a bonus - our referral program rewards great connections.
Regular recognition programs - so your great work never goes unnoticed.
Modern tools and tech - making your job smoother, not harder.
A streamlined onboarding process - hit the ground running with confidence.
A company that invests in long-term relationships, not quick wins.




Disclaimers:

This position does not use artificial intelligence during the hiring process.

Title: Azure Data Analyst
Description:
Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture


Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.


Azure & Databricks Development Support


Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.


Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.


SQL & Data Validation


Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.


Agile Delivery & Collaboration


Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.


Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills


Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).


Business & Domain Skills

Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture

  • Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
  • Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
  • Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
  • Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.

Azure & Databricks Development Support

  • Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
  • Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
  • Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.

Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement

  • Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
  • Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
  • Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.

SQL & Data Validation

  • Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
  • Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
  • Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.

Agile Delivery & Collaboration

  • Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
  • Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
  • Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
  • Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.

Communication & Stakeholder Management

  • Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
  • Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
  • Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills

  • Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
  • Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
  • Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
  • Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).

Business & Domain Skills

  • Strong Business Analysis skills, including requirements elicitation and documentation.
  • Solid knowledge of personal banking products and data domains.
  • Ability to translate business needs into scalable and reusable data designs.

Tools & Ways of Working

  • Experience using Jira for backlog and sprint management.
  • Proficient in Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing.
  • Demonstrated experience working in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.

Soft Skills

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration abilities.
  • Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
  • Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to team goals.

  • Experience in regulated financial services environments.
  • Exposure to data governance, metadata management, or data lineage tools.
  • Familiarity with reporting or downstream analytics use cases.
