Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
In a changing world, unprecedented challenges require unmatched talent. Join one of Montreal’s Top Employers in 2025. We are a dynamic and growing organization having its main establishment located in downtown Montreal and part of a leading international banking institution fully committed to building a more sustainable future. Note that the position may be in the Canadian Branch of BNP Paribas or in one of its subsidiaries based in Montreal.

Internship program

As an intern, you will benefit from a 4 month program that aims to give you the tools to develop yourself and discover your potential through customized trainings, networking sessions and transversal projects.

More information about the program available on our website:

BNP Paribas in Canada Students & Young graduates

The position at a glance

As an intern, you will assist the Head of Territory (HoT) and the COO with data collection, ad-hoc analyses, and special tasks, preparing presentations and documents for meetings, and supporting discussions with Operations to enhance reports related to Canadian client business.

In detail

This role will allow you to actively participate in developing data analysis and business reporting capabilities within the Canada CEO & COO Office. You will gain cross-functional knowledge of the various businesses within the Group and develop valuable skills:


Data Analysis: You will use and enhance your data analysis skills with tools like Excel, PowerBI, Tableau, and ThinkCell.
Project Management: You will learn to manage projects and priorities, monitor progress, report issues, develop action plans.
Communication Skills: You will develop strong communication skills by presenting your findings to senior leadership and preparing detailed business reports.
Professional Network: You will collaborate with multiple teams across the Bank, opening doors to future opportunities.
Career Development: You will collaborate with multiple teams within the Bank, opening potentially leading to various career paths



Work conditions: This position provides for standard working conditions in an office and a normal work schedule from Monday to Friday.

The Strengths And Skills That Will Help You Succeed


Student in a bachelor’s degree in business administration, communication, or other similar field of study
Knowledge of Powerpoint
Capacity to work with large dataset
Knowledge of English and French, written and spoken*
Given the vast majority of our clients, both internal and external, are based outside of Quebec and Canada, specific language requirements may apply. Professional working proficiency in French and English languages are required.



What’s In It For You

In addition to competitive compensation, hybrid work arrangements are available for most positions. In-office presence is required a minimum of 3 days per week, one of which must be on a Monday and/or a Friday. BNP Paribas provides excellent training and personal development programs, as well as opportunities for career development within the company and internationally.

What You Need To Know


We will review candidates as they apply, so don’t wait to submit your application;
BNP Paribas is committed to accessibility and inclusion. During the recruitment process, accommodation needs are available at all times for candidates. You will have the chance to make a request for an accommodation during your application.
You must be legally eligible to work in the Greater Montreal area and, if applicable, hold a valid work or study permit. Physical presence in BNP Paribas’ office(s) is an essential function of this position;
If you are applying and accepted to a position which requires working in/for the U.S. securities industry, you will be required to provide your fingerprints and undergo additional background checks by the FBI. BNP Paribas Securities Corporation is required to maintain a supervisory program over the conduct of its Associated Persons; some of your personal data will be transmitted to the United States of America and made available to US regulators. Please reach out to BNPP for additional information; or you can also find an overview here: 3110. Supervision FINRA.org



Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DE&I) at the heart of our commitments


At BNP Paribas all employees are on an equal footing allowing us to create a work environment that values and respects people for their talents, skills and competences.
BNP Paribas recruits, employs, trains, compensates and promotes regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, sex, disability, age, and other protected status (Employment Equity Act and Canadian Human Right Act).
To learn more about our DE&I commitments, click here



About Us

BNP Paribas is the top bank in the European Union and a major international banking establishment. Present in 63 countries, with more than 180,000 employees, the bank holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services.

BNP Paribas’ mission is to contribute to a responsible and sustainable economy by financing and advising its clients according to the highest ethical standards, while striving to respond to essential concerns in terms of the environment, regional development and social inclusion.

Since 1961, BNP Paribas has supported large Canadian companies and institutions in their business development by offering a full range of specialized financial services and investment products.

With over 1,400 employees, BNP Paribas in Canada continues to attract experts from diverse fields as well as ambitious young talent from around the world. We are proud to offer our employees a rewarding and international workplace where they can build their professional careers by honing their skills, meeting challenges and enriching their knowledge of the financial industry.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LayerZero

The Future is Omnichain.

Founded in 2021, LayerZero’s vision is to create a community of cross-chain developers, building dApps that are no longer constrained by individual blockchain capabilities. With LayerZero's simple, generic messaging protocol, builders will develop cross-chain dApps designed to unify the power of individual blockchains.

We are funded by the best investors in the world including:

a16z, Sequoia, PayPal, Binance Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Uniswap Labs, Circle Ventures, Delphi Digital, and many more.

About The Role

As we continue to grow at LayerZero, we're faced with an exciting set of challenges: more data, more developers, more teams, more projects, and more opportunities for innovation. To tackle these problems head-on, we're expanding our data team with a dedicated Data Analyst.

As a Data Analyst at LayerZero, you'll be at the forefront of shaping a rich data foundation for a company that's making a real impact in the web3 space. You'll work closely with teams and leaders to uncover insights, drive decision-making, and fuel our next-generation products and services.

What You'll Do

As a key member of the data team at LayerZero, you'll be diving headfirst into the world of crypto data - exploring on-chain wallets and contracts, block and transaction data, insights from in-house systems, and third-party intelligence. Your mission will be to combine these diverse datasets into rich, actionable data products for a broad group of stakeholders.


Leveraging and expanding our ever-growing Kimball dimensional model.
Writing SQL to create and expand insights in our in-house reporting solutions.
Collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to conduct ad-hoc explorations and analytics - from engineering questions to help understand production issues to product insights that illuminate the broader crypto landscape.
Be a key owner of data quality, building out insights that serve the data team itself to help guide our data efforts towards cleaner and richer data.
Compose pipelines by writing SQL code to clean, combine, refine, and aggregate data into the insights the organization needs.
Collaborating on new datasets to ingest into our Snowflake data warehouse, working closely with data engineers on your team.
Not afraid of pushing code that supports tens of billions of dollars in daily transaction volume.


Who You Are

We are looking for someone with previous data analyst experience. Likely with a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics or related field, but we also consider and highly value equivalent practical experience.

Required


Strong SQL knowledge and experience
Proven track record in data modeling, statistics, and analytics
Experience working with a broad range of stakeholders
Strong convictions weakly held.
You enjoy working in a fast-paced start-up environment, where you'll need to take initiative and solve problems independently.
You understand the importance of data quality through incremental data development.


Nice to have


Experience with general programming, we use Python and Go on the data stack
Experience with Snowflake
Experience building DAG based data pipelines
Experience with streaming real-time data pipelines
Previous experience with blockchain technologies, smart contracts, and decentralized finance
Experience with Kimball dimensional modeling
Working on a mid-to-large scale data stacks


Equal Opportunity Employer

LayerZero Labs is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. LayerZero Labs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Konrad is a next-generation digital consultancy. We are dedicated to solving complex business problems for our global clients with creative and forward-thinking solutions. Our employees enjoy a culture built on innovation and a commitment to creating best-in-class digital products in use by hundreds of millions of consumers around the world. We hire exceptionally smart, analytical, and hard-working people who are lifelong learners.

About The Role

This role sits on our consulting team, where your internal title will be Consultant, working primarily in a data analytics capacity across client engagements.

As a Consultant focused on Data Analytics, you are an early-to-mid career data professional with a strong foundation in analysis and a desire to grow both your technical and consulting skill set. Leveraging your analytics background, you will support the delivery of digital products for our clients through analytics and reporting solutions that help organizations better understand their data and make informed decisions.

In addition to your analytics work, you will gain exposure to broader areas of digital consulting, including digital product development, digital transformation, and user experience (UX/CX). You are interested in understanding how data supports product decisions, customer experiences, and overall business strategy.

You will work closely with senior analysts, data scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, and business stakeholders across a variety of client engagements. With guidance and mentorship, you will contribute to analytics initiatives including dashboarding, exploratory analysis, KPI tracking, and data quality validation, while also building broader consulting skills across areas such as project management, user experience, and design thinking.

You are curious, detail-oriented, and motivated to continuously improve your analytical and consulting capabilities.

What You’ll Do


Support client and internal teams in understanding business questions and translating them into analytical requirements
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights under the guidance of senior team members Build and maintain dashboards and recurring reports to track key metrics and performance indicators
Assist in documenting metrics, definitions, and analytics processes
Collaborate with data engineers and other analysts to support data modeling and reporting layers Communicate insights clearly through visualizations, summaries, and presentations
Proactively identify opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and analytical workflows


Qualifications


Minimum 2+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or analytics-focused role
Programming experience in Python, Bash scripting, R or related language
Working proficiency in SQL and experience querying cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, BigQuery)
Experience with Business Intelligence and data visualization tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI)
Foundational understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience defining and tracking KPIs and metrics
A/B testing and causal analysis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Clear written and verbal communication skills Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional and consulting-style environments
Interest in learning about data governance, privacy, and security best practices


Perks And Benefits


Comprehensive Health & Wellness Benefits Package
Retirement Planning
Parental Leave Program
Flexible Working Hours
Work from Home Flexibility
Service Recognition Programs
Socials, Outings & Retreats
Culture of Learning & Development


Bonus Points

Have you taken any courses at BrainStation? A lot of our design and development best practices and processes are taught during our courses, make sure to highlight this experience in your cover letter if you have!

Konrad is committed to maintaining a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status will receive consideration for employment. If you have any accessibility requirements or concerns regarding the hiring process or employment with us, please notify us so we can provide suitable accommodation.

The estimated compensation for this position is $75,000 to $100,000. This is an estimate and a compensation offer will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities and alignment with market data.

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

If you have any accessibility requirements or concerns regarding the hiring process or employment with us, please notify us so we can provide suitable accommodation.

The estimated compensation for this position is $75,000 to $100,000. This is an estimate and a compensation offer will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities and alignment with market data.

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We’re searching for a Data Analyst. Are you searching for new possibilities?

Westland Insurance Group Ltd is growing and has an exciting opportunity for an Data Analyst to join our growing team. This hybrid role is based out of our Head Office in Surrey, BC.

Expected Compensation: The expected hiring range for this role is $62,400- $78,000 with a 37.5-hour work week. Your rate of pay will be determined based on your individual qualifications and experience, while considering internal equity. 

Westland also offers a competitive annual short-term incentive program that enables employees to share in the rewards of the business and emphasizes the measures that are key to our success.

Westland is committed to continuous learning where employees can grow their potential and write the stories of their careers. Financial assistance is available for approved work-related training/development courses leading to certification, and programs that offer professional development, including seminars and conferences.

Discover What’s Possible, With Westland.

The Westland story is all about growth, and that means plenty of possibility for everyone on our team. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of clients, communities, and each other with equal commitment. As we continue to open new doors, we're inviting amazing people like you to join us.

As a Data Analyst, you’ll have the unique opportunity to lay the foundation for how Westland can leverage data to make important decisions.

Interested in learning more?

Why You'll Love Westland


An open, flexible and welcoming workplace
Plenty of opportunities to grow and learn
Autonomy to drive your own success
Be part of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds
A total rewards program that takes care of your financial, physical, and mental health
Flexible paid Values Days to celebrate days important to you
Paid Volunteer Days for employees as part of Amplifying Communities, a program to support causes that matter most to our employees and clients


Why We’ll Love You


You strive to understand the data and love seeking ways to improve the quality of the data and reporting
You enjoy collaborating with others to identify and prioritize use cases for analysis and build out models to answer business questions
You love numbers and have a strong background in math, statistics or equivalent such as a bachelor’s degree in those areas or related fields
You can translate complex information and findings for business leaders
At least two years’ experience in a data analytics role
Previous experience with data analysis and modeling using SQL, R, and Python
Bonus: Experience in P&C insurance
Bonus: Experience using data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake or Microsoft Fabric Bonus: Experience with Git based version control on platforms such as GitHub or Azure DevOps


Once Here, You'll


Be a contributing member of our inclusive culture, alongside an extraordinarily talented community of people with a wide variety of backgrounds.
Working with IT and others to design and improve the quality of our data and reporting by understanding current and future data analysis needs, business opportunities and risks.
Continuously seek out and identify areas to improve data reporting practices, data quality, and processes
Prepare and present findings to diverse audiences, including partners


Want to get to know each other better? Send your resume our way.

You belong here

We strive to be much more than a great place to work. Here, you join a collective of incredibly vibrant, purpose driven, and open-minded people. Every day, Westland proves that building a great business means taking care of communities, clients, and each other with equal commitment. You will be supported and respected for who you are and for the voice you add to the conversation.

At Westland, you will feel the power of community. Westland Insurance Ltd. is an inclusive organization that values diversity in its workforce. We listen, champion equity and diversity, and create safe welcoming spaces where everyone has a voice and is free to be themselves. Westland Insurance Ltd. encourages applications from all qualified individuals and will accommodate applicants' disability-related needs, up to the point of undue hardship, throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.

If you require a disability-related accommodation in order to participate in the recruitment process, please contact the recruitment team by email at careers@westlandinsurance.ca

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
Be part of a community of authentic, proud and trusted people



At UAP, we believe we don’t just deliver car and heavy vehicle parts all around the country; we also help keep Canadians moving forward. This makes for a special kind of workplace. We have the experience and strength of our 100-year legacy and the desire to always do better for our team members and clients. Our 5000 colleagues proudly serve thousands of customers every day across Canada.



If the hat fits, we’d be proud to have you wear it! Send us your resume and join a people-centric company with a reputation of excellence.





Job Description



We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst, Business Intelligence, to play a key role in advancing our data culture and analytical capabilities.



We are looking for a curious, adaptable, and forward-thinking individual who is comfortable working in a constantly evolving technology environment, including the integration of AI-assisted development practices.



Key Responsibilities



Act as a strategic business partner to understand organizational challenges and define performance indicators aligned with business priorities.
Translate business needs into comprehensive analytical solutions (semantic models, standardized datasets, dashboards, and advanced analytics) that support decision-making.
Design, maintain, and optimize the Power BI architecture (star schema modeling, advanced DAX, security, deployment, and incremental refresh).
Optimize data workflows using SQL and Google BigQuery, and leverage Python for advanced analytics and automation.
Define business intelligence development standards and ensure proper documentation of solutions to guarantee quality and sustainability.
Plan and prioritize business intelligence initiatives based on business value, while ensuring quality, timely delivery, and strategic alignment of deliverables.


Qualifications


Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Minimum of five (5) years of experience in data analysis or business intelligence within a corporate environment.
Strong expertise in Power BI (star schema modeling, advanced DAX, security, and deployment).
Excellent proficiency in SQL and experience with Google BigQuery.
Solid proficiency in Python for advanced analytics and automation.
Ability to understand business challenges and translate requirements into structured analytical solutions.
Excellent communication skills in both French and English, spoken and written. In this role, you will regularly collaborate with internal clients across Canada.


Additional Information



UAP is a diverse community that promotes inclusion and respect. The sum of our individual differences, experiences, knowledge, self-expression, unique capabilities and talents represents the richness of our culture. UAP is committed to employment equity and encourages applications from women, visible minorities and people with disabilities. By valuing a diverse workforce, we ensure that our hiring practices are fair and equitable.

Title: Power Platform Data Analyst
Description:
English version below / Usage de l'anglais requis pour échanger avec collègues anglophones et relations d'affaires avec d'autres provinces / US.




Offre d’emploi – Power BI / Power Platform Analyst

Localisation : Montréal

Type de contrat : Permanent

Mode de travail : Hybride – 3 jours/semaine au bureau

Salaire : Jusqu’à 95 000 $

Le masculin est utilisé pour alléger l’annonce.




🚀 Ce que nous allons accomplir ensemble

Vous rejoignez une équipe technologique stratégique, au cœur des initiatives d’optimisation des processus, d’automatisation et de GenAI.

Votre impact sera concret : rendre la donnée exploitable, lisible et utile à l’échelle de l’entreprise grâce à des rapports fiables, harmonisés et orientés décision.




🎯 Votre mission (si vous l’acceptez !)

En tant que Power BI / Power Platform Analyst, vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la conception et l’évolution de solutions de reporting utilisées par des équipes multidisciplinaires, dans un environnement structuré et exigeant.




📅 Votre quotidien sera rythmé par :

Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord Power BI performants et intuitifs 📊
Traduire les besoins d’affaires en modèles de données clairs et exploitables
Développer et optimiser des solutions sur la Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)
Rédiger une documentation solide : sources de données, règles de gestion, définitions
Collaborer avec des parties prenantes réparties sur plusieurs sites 🌍
Former et accompagner les utilisateurs finaux pour maximiser l’adoption
Améliorer en continu la qualité, la performance et la pertinence des rapports




👥 Votre future équipe

Vous évoluerez au sein de l’équipe Fleet Reporting, rattachée à la division Technology COO – Process Optimization, dans un contexte data-driven, enterprise et hautement collaboratif.




🧰 Votre stack de jeu

Power BI (Desktop & Service)
DAX & Power Query
Power Platform : Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse
SQL (extraction, transformation, modélisation)
Bonnes pratiques en BI & data modeling




🧠 Vous êtes la bonne personne si :

Vous avez 5+ ans d’expérience professionnelle, dont 3+ ans en BI / Data Analytics
Vous êtes hands-on et aimez être dans le concret (profil IC senior, non managérial)
Vous êtes à l’aise dans des environnements grandes entreprises / réglementés
Vous savez vulgariser la donnée et raconter une histoire claire avec des chiffres 📈
Vous aimez collaborer avec des interlocuteurs variés et documenter votre travail




🌟 Atouts appréciés

Expérience en finance ou services financiers
Intérêt pour les sujets GenAI & automatisation
Connaissance de Snowflake (Cortex = gros plus)




🎁 Les avantages Astek

Jusqu’à 4 semaines de congés + 5 jours mobiles
Assurances collectives complètes (télémédecine & PAE inclus)
Remboursement du transport en commun 🚇
Forfait cellulaire & matériel informatique 💻
Allocation sport / bien-être 🏃 ♀️
Programme de cooptation
Activités sociales & team buildings 🎉




🤝 Rencontrons-nous !

Vous vous reconnaissez dans ce rôle ?

Postulez directement et Sara, Spécialiste en Acquisition de Talents, vous contactera pour un premier échange.

Vous rencontrerez ensuite Floriane, votre futur manager, pour discuter du poste et de vos ambitions.




-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Job Offer – Power BI / Power Platform Analyst 📊

Location: Montreal

Contract: Permanent

Work model: Hybrid – 3 days/week in office 🏢

Salary: Up to $95K




🚀 What we’ll achieve together

You’ll join a strategic technology team driving process optimization, automation, and GenAI initiatives through enterprise-level reporting.

Your work will directly support decision-making by turning complex data into clear, actionable insights.




🎯 Your mission

As a Power BI / Power Platform Analyst, you will design, evolve, and support reporting solutions used across the organization in a structured, data-driven environment.




📅 Your day-to-day

Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports
Translate business requirements into robust data models and insights
Develop solutions using the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse)
Document data sources, logic, and definitions thoroughly
Collaborate with stakeholders across locations 🌍
Train and support end users to drive adoption
Continuously improve report quality and performance




🧰 Your tech stack

Power BI (Desktop & Service)
DAX & Power Query
Power Platform
SQL
BI & data modeling best practices




🧠 You’re a great fit if you:

Have 5+ years of experience, including 3+ years in BI / Analytics
Are a senior hands-on individual contributor
Are comfortable in large, regulated enterprise environments
Communicate clearly and tell compelling stories with data
Enjoy collaboration and documentation




⭐ Nice to have

Experience in finance, investment banking, or financial services
Strong interest in GenAI and automation initiatives
Experience with Snowflake (Cortex is a strong plus)




🎁 Why join Astek Canada?

Up to 4 weeks of vacation + 5 personal days
Comprehensive group insurance (telemedicine & EAP included)
Public transit reimbursement 🚇
Cell phone plan & IT equipment reimbursement 💻
Annual wellness / sports allowance 🏃 ♂️
Referral bonus program
Regular social events & team activities 🎉




🤝 Let’s meet!

If this opportunity speaks to you, apply directly.

Sara, Talent Acquisition Specialist, will reach out for an initial conversation.

You will then meet Floriane, your future manager, to discuss the role, your background, and your career goals.

Title: Manager, Data Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 259823

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Manager, Data Analyst will work with Canadian Wealth Management Data Operations to provide data-driven solutions to various Wealth business applications data. This position involves maintaining data extraction, transformation processes, and reporting development to support regulatory, compliance and ongoing business operations.

Is this role right for you? In this role you will:


Work closely with the Canadian Wealth Management Operations team to support day-to-day reporting activities
Deliver the daily scheduled reports and support Adhoc reporting requirements with complete data analysis and provide the technical solution to meet the customer requirements
Work with data investigation, analysis, develop and maintain a strong knowledge of wealth management business workflow to deliver the expected solution
As a Senior Business Analyst Specialist who ensures re-engineering, you will work with the business to understand existing functionalities, and develop new solutions, and write scripts for analysis, testing and regression, understanding relationship database structure and how the data maps to various business application
Identify programming / analysis skill gaps and keep up with the new data analysis tools and programming software to consistently improve self and existing processes.
Be responsible for performing heavy data-driven analytics and reconciliations for complex calculation logic in various wealth application data which is highly statistical and computation intensive


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? – We’d love to work with you if you have:


A University degree in quantitative discipline (mathematics, science, computer science, or engineering)
A minimum of 5 years of industry and business analysis experience
An understanding in wealth business management
Data Knowledge in wealth or financial related business
In-depth knowledge of relational database concepts
In-depth knowledge of Data analytics and Business intelligence
SQL, AS400, Microsoft Access, Microsoft VBA, or VB .Net, and Sybase
Experience using Power BI, Python, Databricks is an asset
Experience with GCP is an asset
Experience in AI is an asset
The ability to adapt to change quickly and adjust work accordingly in a positive manner
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Description:
Build something people love

Wealthsimple's mission is to help everyone achieve financial freedom – by making financial services simple, transparent, and low-cost. We're Canada's largest fintech, trusted by over 3 million clients with more than $100 billion in assets. We move fast, we own our work, and we care deeply about the people using our products. If that sounds like you, keep reading.

We're proud of what we've built — and we're just getting started. Read our Culture Manual and learn more about how we work.

Internships @ Wealthsimple

At Wealthsimple, we offer 4 to 8-month internships that are open to co-op and non-co-op students, and recent grads. During your internship, you will have the opportunity to contribute to projects that are changing the landscape of financial services for Canadians. You will be on a team that supports your growth, provides mentorship, and connects you to the broader Wealthsimple community!

Our internship program follows a hybrid work model, where you’ll be working from our Toronto headquarters on Wednesdays and Thursdays. This structure offers the flexibility of remote work, while also providing the collaboration, connection, and mentorship that come from being together in person.

Eligibility

✔ Currently enrolled as a student at a Canadian post-secondary institution or technical bootcamp

✔ New graduates - Within 6 months of your graduation date

✔ Available to work full-time hours

✔ Residing in Canada

✔ Able to commute to our Toronto HQ on Wednesdays and Thursdays

✔ At least 2 paid work terms completed

✔ Legally authorized to work in Canada (Wealthsimple does not provide immigration or relocation support for interns)

Application Deadline: May 19th, 2026 9:00AM ET

Data @ Wealthsimple

Data Science at Wealthsimple turns complex, real-world financial data into decisions, products, and experiences that help millions build wealth. We partner closely with Product, Engineering, and business leaders to design experiments, build models, and surface insights that shape what we ship — from client-facing features to growth strategies and risk policies. We give teams the insights, tools, and decision frameworks they need to ship impactful work — ensuring we’re building data-informed products our clients trust and love.

About The Teams

Risk & Financial Decision Science

Helps Wealthsimple grow responsibly by combining experimentation, modeling, and domain expertise to shape credit, fraud, and financial risk policies. This team turns complex, real-world data into clear recommendations — from detecting suspicious activity to understanding how economic conditions affect our products. If you're passionate about keeping a fast-moving fintech both safe and scalable, this is your team.

Product Decision Science

Partners closely with Product, Engineering, and Design to decide what we build, how we launch it, and how we measure success. This team designs experiments, defines metrics, and builds data products that explain how clients actually use Wealthsimple — validating ideas, iterating quickly, and doubling down on what works. If you're passionate about using data to tell clear stories and ship better product experiences, this is your team.

Growth Decision Science

Focuses on how Wealthsimple grows — from acquisition and onboarding to engagement, retention, and cross-sell across our product suite. This team builds models and insights that power personalization, campaign strategy, and lifecycle optimization to grow net deposits sustainably. If you're passionate about finding leverage in funnels, experiments, and growth loops — and seeing your work show up in real business outcomes — this is your team.

Who We're Looking For


Take ownership without being asked — they see something broken and fix it
Ship fast and know what to cut
Learn on the go and ask for help when they need it
Use AI tools in their workflow and are curious about what else is possible
Care about the person on the other end of what they're building
Believe that debate, inclusivity, and transparency make for better products


Our Tech Stack


Python-first environment with Pandas, scikit-learn, NumPy, and Jupyter
SQL for data access and manipulation across large, production datasets
Statistical foundations across both frequentist and Bayesian approaches
Machine learning toolkit spanning regression, decision trees, and beyond
Experimentation infrastructure for A/B testing and causal inference at scale


The Interview Process

Applications close on May 19th, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

Step 1 — Application + Video Submission: Apply and submit a short video response with a written transcript. Applications open May 4–17.

Step 2 — (May 19th-May 29th) Technical Interview: A 1-hour interview in Python with a technical interviewer.

Step 3 — (June 1st-June 12th) - Project Review Interview: A 30-minute conversation with one of our engineering managers about how you work and what drives you.

Step 4 — Offers start to be extended the week of June 8th

Why Wealthsimple?

🤑 Competitive salary with group savings matching plan using Wealthsimple for Business

🌴 Generous vacation days, wellness days and unlimited sick & mental health days

🎉 Intern programming including educational workshops, hackathons, and mentorship

🌎 A wide variety of peer and company-led Employee Resource Groups (ie. Rainbow, Inclusive Gender Network, Black @ WS)

What past interns have said about their experience at Wealthsimple:

"I absolutely loved the term here and had a great chance to learn new things and meet many amazingly talented people! I also really appreciated the mentorship program, having someone who was an alumni from my school+program was a great chance to learn more and help plan my career and academic journey!"

"A lot of autonomy and trust from teammates; In-person office experience allowed for a lot of deeper friendships; I genuinely like the Wealthsimple product which made it very exciting to work on it"

"The other interns were really a highlight as well as all of the things that y'all planned for us. I also would say my own team really made me feel at home and welcomed. I love the codebase and engineering tools a lot and will miss it!"

With over 1,000 employees coast to coast in North America, be a part of our Canadian success story and help shape the financial future of millions — join us!

Why Wealthsimple?

🌸 Top-tier health benefits and life insurance

📈 Long-term group savings with employer match, through Wealthsimple for Business

🌴 20 vacation days, 4 wellness days, and unlimited sick and mental health days per year

✈️ 90 days away: work outside Canada for up to 90 days per year

👥 Employee resource groups, including Rainbow (2SLGBTQ), Women of WS, and Black at WS

🌎 We are a hybrid team with over 1,500 employees across North America. The people are one of the best parts of working here: you'll collaborate with incredibly talented, curious, and driven teammates who are deeply committed to doing great work.

ICYMI

Technology & Innovation at Wealthsimple: We move quickly and build thoughtfully. That means we're always looking for better ways to work — whether that's new tools, AI, or rethinking how we approach a problem. We don't expect you to have all the answers, but we do expect curiosity and a willingness to evolve alongside the products we're building.

Inclusion Statement: We're building products for a diverse world, and we need a diverse team to do it well. We strongly encourage applications from everyone, regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status.

Accessibility Statement: We're committed to an accessible hiring experience. If you need any accommodations throughout the interview process, please let us know — we'll work with you to make sure you have what you need. We also welcome any feedback on how we can better accommodate candidates with accessibility needs.

AI in Hiring: We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of our hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our team but don't replace human judgment – all final hiring decisions are made by people. If you have questions about how your data is used, reach out to us.

Compensation Range: CA$74K - CA$90K

Title: Data Analyst, Credit Risk Management Department
Company: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada)
Description:
Department / Branch: Credit Risk Management Department

Reports To: Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department

Employment Type: Full-Time Contract/ Permanent

Location: Richmond Hill Office







Responsibilities

1. Model Development and Optimization

Design, develop, and optimize credit rating models to solve business problems.
Experiment with algorithms, architectures, and frameworks to improve model performance.
Conduct regular validation and tuning of models for accuracy and efficiency.

2. Data Analysis

Work with large datasets, ensuring quality, consistency, and relevance for training models.
Identify patterns and insights to refine model inputs and features.

3. Model Evaluation and Testing

Implement evaluation metrics and validation techniques to ensure model reliability.
Perform testing to compare different models and configurations.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

Deploy models to production environments and monitor their real-time performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues to maintain model robustness over time.
Conduct model performance monitoring and reporting.

5. Collaboration and Communication

Collaborate with data stakeholders to align model objectives with business goals.
Communicate findings, methodologies, and results to technical and non-technical audiences .or regulator.

6. Other works assigned by Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department.




Qualifications:

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering or related fields.
At least 3 years of experience in financial modelling, preferably within banking or financial services sector.
Strong knowledge in model development, validation, evaluation and optimization.
Proficient in Python, R or similar programming languages.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English with work proficiency.
Fluency in Chinese is an asset.




Recruitment Process, Privacy and Accessibility Notice

We thank all applicants for their interest in career opportunities with Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada) (“ICBK”). Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contact

ed. ICBK is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible recruitment process. If you require accommodation at any stage of the application or interview process, please inform us and we will work with you to address your needs.

For more information on our accessibility commitment, please refer to Accessibility at ICBK on our corporate website. To learn more about how ICBK collects, uses, and safeguards personal information provided during the recruitment process, please refer to the ICBK Privacy Statement for Recruitment and Employment available on our corporate website.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Technology

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Greater Montreal Metropolitan Area, Canada

Reporting To: Software Engineering Manager

Description

Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la conception, de la création et de la maintenance de la couche analytique ainsi que des rapports destinés aux clients, reposant sur la plateforme Compliance Pro.

Au sein de l’équipe Reporting, ce rôle consiste à traduire des exigences complexes en matière de conformité et de sécurité en tableaux de bord robustes et en rapports tabulaires qui répondent aux besoins des utilisateurs finaux. Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la livraison de bout en bout : modélisation des données, création et mise à jour des tableaux de bord et des widgets, définition des meilleures représentations des données, ainsi que garantie de l’exactitude, de la performance et de la cohérence des rapports avec les données affichées dans CPro.

Ce rôle est fortement orienté client : une grande partie du travail consiste à créer et mettre à jour des rapports personnalisés pour des opérateurs spécifiques, à résoudre des problèmes en production et à s’assurer que les rapports critiques pour l’entreprise sont fiables et à jour.

Construire des solutions innovantes ; permettre des lieux de travail plus sûrs pour tous.

Nous allons créer un monde du travail plus sûr, en développant des logiciels pour soutenir un réseau mondial d'acheteurs, de fournisseurs et de partenaires responsables.

Aujourd'hui, chez Veriforce, nous soulageons plus de 50 000 organisations à travers le monde des contraintes de conformité, en les aidant à protéger leurs employés, leurs activités et la planète.

Nous veillons à ce que notre réseau de clients, de fournisseurs et de sous-traitants respecte les normes les plus importantes, qu'il s'agisse de santé, de sécurité, de développement durable ou de comportement éthique, en élaborant les meilleures solutions du marché.

La technologie que nous construisons aujourd'hui créera un avenir meilleur.

{Insérer une déclaration d'impact tirée du dossier d'appel d'offres.}

Anglais De Niveau Avancé

Cette exigence est requise puisque dans le cadre de cette fonction, vous aurez à intervenir auprès des clients, partenaires et/ou nos filiales internationaux sur une base récurrente tant à l’oral qu’à l’écrit.

Dans le présent document, le genre masculin est utilisé dans le seul but d'alléger le texte

Qu'est-ce Que Cela Signifie Au Jour Le Jour


Créer et améliorer des tableaux de bord et des rapports spécifiques aux clients
Assurer la mise en valeur des données (« data storytelling ») afin que celles-ci soient pertinentes et exploitables pour la prise de décision
Veiller à ce que les rapports Sisense reflètent fidèlement les données de l’application en production, tant en termes de valeurs que de mise en forme
Diagnostiquer et résoudre les incidents en production
Collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, les équipes QA, les parties prenantes internes et les équipes en contact avec les clients afin d’affiner les critères d’acceptation, clarifier la logique des données et valider les rapports nouveaux ou mis à jour
Mettre en place et maintenir des standards de reporting cohérents à travers l’environnement Sisense et les produits de reporting
Collaborer avec les équipes d’ingénierie et de données pour identifier et modéliser de nouvelles sources de données lorsque nécessaire
Maintenir une documentation technique claire afin de faciliter la maintenance et l’intégration des nouveaux collaborateurs
Participer aux rituels agiles, utiliser Jira pour gérer les tâches et fournir des estimations, des mises à jour d’avancement et des livrables clairs pour le QAT


Ce Dont Vous Aurez Besoin Pour Réussir


Expérience pratique dans le développement de solutions analytiques avec Sisense (ou une plateforme BI similaire), incluant la création de tableaux de bord complexes et de rapports tabulaires avec filtres, widgets et champs personnalisés
Solides compétences en SQL et expérience en modélisation de données pour le reporting (jointures, agrégations, champs calculés, conversions de types de données, optimisation des performances)
Expérience démontrée dans la gestion de rapports critiques en environnement de production, incluant :
L’analyse des écarts entre les systèmes sources et les données produites
La correction des problèmes de données et de mise en forme
La maintenance de rapports opérationnels à fort enjeu
Capacité à interpréter des spécifications métiers détaillées et à les traduire en solutions de reporting précises
Excellentes compétences en « data storytelling » et solide compréhension des bonnes pratiques en reporting
Expérience dans l’analyse et l’amélioration de rapports Sisense existants
À l’aise pour collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, le QAT et les équipes en contact avec les clients, notamment pour valider le comportement des tableaux de bord, des filtres et des résultats
Grande attention aux détails et souci de la qualité
Excellentes capacités d’analyse et de résolution de problèmes, avec aptitude à identifier des enjeux à travers plusieurs couches de données


Ce Que Vous Obtiendrez En Retour

Nous voulons que vous puissiez donner le meilleur de vous-même. Nous mettons l'accent sur les moyens de soutenir notre équipe pour qu'elle donne le meilleur d'elle-même. Voici quelques-uns des avantages que nous offrons :

Santé et bien-être personnel / Avantages

>🧠 Soutien intégré à la santé mentale et au bien-être

>🌴 Vacances - à partir de 3 semaines

>💟 Journées du bien-être et journée annuelle de dons - une occasion supplémentaire de se rendre service ou de rendre service à la communauté

>🩺 Couverture médicale et dentaire complète

>😴 Fin de l'année, fermeture de l'entreprise pour vous permettre de vous détendre et de vous ressourcer

Planification future

>🏫 Licence d'apprentissage LinkedIn pour la mise à niveau et le développement

Vous êtes intéressé(e) mais ne pensez pas répondre à toutes les exigences ?

Notre équipe de recrutement évalue et examine toutes les candidatures en fonction du poste et des besoins de l'entreprise. Nous croyons que les gens ont des compétences transférables et non techniques et nous voulons que vous sachiez que nous prenons en considération les cas où une personne ne répond pas à tous les critères, mais possède néanmoins les aptitudes et les capacités nécessaires. Notre priorité est de veiller à ce que les candidats soient en mesure de réussir. Nous prendrons une décision finale en déterminant si nous pouvons offrir le soutien nécessaire pour améliorer les compétences ou fournir le soutien au développement dont vous avez besoin pour tirer le meilleur parti de cette opportunité avec nous !

Apportez votre entièreté au travail.

Veriforce est fière d'être un employeur qui respecte l'égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à ce qu'aucun candidat ne soit discriminé en raison de son identité et de son expression sexuelles, de sa race, de son handicap, de son ethnicité, de son orientation sexuelle, de son âge, de sa couleur, de sa région, de sa croyance, de son origine nationale ou de son sexe. Nous sommes déterminés à développer une équipe diversifiée tout en continuant à créer un environnement inclusif où chacun se sent en sécurité et a la possibilité d'être lui-même.

Ce à quoi vous pouvez vous attendre si vous postulez :


Une réponse à votre candidature dans les 15 jours ouvrables
Un processus d'entretien comprenant
Un appel initial de découverte avec le recruteur
Un premier entretien via Microsoft Teams
Un entretien supplémentaire (probablement en face à face) avec les parties prenantes avec lesquelles vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration dans le cadre de votre fonction.

Nous tenons à ce que notre processus de recrutement vous permette de donner le meilleur de vous-même. Si vous souhaitez que nous procédions à des ajustements, n'hésitez pas à nous en faire part.

  • Créer et améliorer des tableaux de bord et des rapports spécifiques aux clients
  • Assurer la mise en valeur des données (« data storytelling ») afin que celles-ci soient pertinentes et exploitables pour la prise de décision
  • Veiller à ce que les rapports Sisense reflètent fidèlement les données de l’application en production, tant en termes de valeurs que de mise en forme
  • Diagnostiquer et résoudre les incidents en production
  • Collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, les équipes QA, les parties prenantes internes et les équipes en contact avec les clients afin d’affiner les critères d’acceptation, clarifier la logique des données et valider les rapports nouveaux ou mis à jour
  • Mettre en place et maintenir des standards de reporting cohérents à travers l’environnement Sisense et les produits de reporting
  • Collaborer avec les équipes d’ingénierie et de données pour identifier et modéliser de nouvelles sources de données lorsque nécessaire
  • Maintenir une documentation technique claire afin de faciliter la maintenance et l’intégration des nouveaux collaborateurs
  • Participer aux rituels agiles, utiliser Jira pour gérer les tâches et fournir des estimations, des mises à jour d’avancement et des livrables clairs pour le QAT

Ce Dont Vous Aurez Besoin Pour Réussir

  • Expérience pratique dans le développement de solutions analytiques avec Sisense (ou une plateforme BI similaire), incluant la création de tableaux de bord complexes et de rapports tabulaires avec filtres, widgets et champs personnalisés
  • Solides compétences en SQL et expérience en modélisation de données pour le reporting (jointures, agrégations, champs calculés, conversions de types de données, optimisation des performances)
  • Expérience démontrée dans la gestion de rapports critiques en environnement de production, incluant :
  • L’analyse des écarts entre les systèmes sources et les données produites
  • La correction des problèmes de données et de mise en forme
  • La maintenance de rapports opérationnels à fort enjeu
  • Capacité à interpréter des spécifications métiers détaillées et à les traduire en solutions de reporting précises
  • Excellentes compétences en « data storytelling » et solide compréhension des bonnes pratiques en reporting
  • Expérience dans l’analyse et l’amélioration de rapports Sisense existants
  • À l’aise pour collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, le QAT et les équipes en contact avec les clients, notamment pour valider le comportement des tableaux de bord, des filtres et des résultats
  • Grande attention aux détails et souci de la qualité
  • Excellentes capacités d’analyse et de résolution de problèmes, avec aptitude à identifier des enjeux à travers plusieurs couches de données

Ce Que Vous Obtiendrez En Retour

Nous voulons que vous puissiez donner le meilleur de vous-même. Nous mettons l'accent sur les moyens de soutenir notre équipe pour qu'elle donne le meilleur d'elle-même. Voici quelques-uns des avantages que nous offrons :

Santé et bien-être personnel / Avantages

>🧠 Soutien intégré à la santé mentale et au bien-être

>🌴 Vacances - à partir de 3 semaines

>💟 Journées du bien-être et journée annuelle de dons - une occasion supplémentaire de se rendre service ou de rendre service à la communauté

>🩺 Couverture médicale et dentaire complète

>😴 Fin de l'année, fermeture de l'entreprise pour vous permettre de vous détendre et de vous ressourcer

Planification future

>🏫 Licence d'apprentissage LinkedIn pour la mise à niveau et le développement

Vous êtes intéressé(e) mais ne pensez pas répondre à toutes les exigences ?

Notre équipe de recrutement évalue et examine toutes les candidatures en fonction du poste et des besoins de l'entreprise. Nous croyons que les gens ont des compétences transférables et non techniques et nous voulons que vous sachiez que nous prenons en considération les cas où une personne ne répond pas à tous les critères, mais possède néanmoins les aptitudes et les capacités nécessaires. Notre priorité est de veiller à ce que les candidats soient en mesure de réussir. Nous prendrons une décision finale en déterminant si nous pouvons offrir le soutien nécessaire pour améliorer les compétences ou fournir le soutien au développement dont vous avez besoin pour tirer le meilleur parti de cette opportunité avec nous !

Apportez votre entièreté au travail.

Veriforce est fière d'être un employeur qui respecte l'égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à ce qu'aucun candidat ne soit discriminé en raison de son identité et de son expression sexuelles, de sa race, de son handicap, de son ethnicité, de son orientation sexuelle, de son âge, de sa couleur, de sa région, de sa croyance, de son origine nationale ou de son sexe. Nous sommes déterminés à développer une équipe diversifiée tout en continuant à créer un environnement inclusif où chacun se sent en sécurité et a la possibilité d'être lui-même.

Ce à quoi vous pouvez vous attendre si vous postulez :

  • Une réponse à votre candidature dans les 15 jours ouvrables
  • Un processus d'entretien comprenant
    • Un appel initial de découverte avec le recruteur
    • Un premier entretien via Microsoft Teams
    • Un entretien supplémentaire (probablement en face à face) avec les parties prenantes avec lesquelles vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration dans le cadre de votre fonction.
Nous tenons à ce que notre processus de recrutement vous permette de donner le meilleur de vous-même. Si vous souhaitez que nous procédions à des ajustements, n'hésitez pas à nous en faire part.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
ID Logistics is a leading international contract logistics company with operations in more than 19 countries worldwide providing innovative supply chain solutions for some of the world’s largest retailers and manufacturers. As the first site in Canada, our focus is on operational excellence, safety, and customer satisfaction, with a strong commitment to teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement. 




ID Logistics Canada – XYY1 in Ajax, ON is looking for an ICQA Data Analyst to join our growing team for three (3) months. Reporting to the Stock Manager the ICQA Data Analyst will be responsible for the following:




Responsibilities:




· Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets related to inventory accuracy, cycle counts, quality trends, and operational performance.
 · Develop daily, weekly, and monthly ICQA reports, dashboards, and KPIs for leadership review.
 · Identify discrepancies, patterns, and root causes impacting inventory accuracy and quality assurance.
 · Support cycle count processes by validating results and tracking variances.
 · Conduct deep-dive investigations into inventory issues, shrink, and process gaps.
 · Partner with Operations Supervisors, Inventory Control, and Quality teams to implement corrective actions.
 · Monitor key ICQA performance metrics such as accuracy %, defect rate, count compliance, and audit results.
 · Maintain data integrity through validation, reconciliation, and error-checking processes.
 · Present insights and findings to management to support data-driven decision making.
 · Support ongoing and ad-hoc ICQA projects during the contract term.




The above responsibilities describe the general nature and level of work being performed. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibility, duties and skills required for this position. Additional duties may be assigned as needed.




Qualifications:




Post-secondary education in Data Analytics, Supply Chain, Business, Mathematics, or related field.
 Proven experience in data analysis, preferably in a warehouse, logistics, 3PL, or fulfillment environment.
 Strong proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, formulas, Power Query an asset).
 Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
 Knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) or inventory software is an asset.
 Understanding of ICQA principles, cycle counts, root-cause analysis, and inventory workflows.
 Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving abilities.
 Ability to communicate complex data insights clearly and concisely.
 Ability to work independently and meet deadlines within a fast-paced environment.




Accessibility & Transparency at ID Logistics Canada
 At ID Logistics Canada, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. We welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and will provide accommodations throughout the recruitment, selection, and employment process upon request. If you require accommodation at any stage, please contact us and we will work with you to meet your needs.




In alignment with Ontario’s Pay Transparency and Employment Standards, ID Logistics Canada provides clear information on compensation and working conditions. We do not require Canadian work experience as a condition of employment and are dedicated to fair, transparent, and respectful hiring practices. Applicants will be informed of the outcome of their application within the required timelines, and all postings and related materials are retained in accordance with provincial legislation.

Title: GCP Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst - Cloud Migration

Remote

4 +months

Video







Role Responsibilities

Here are key job responsibilities for a Data Analyst supporting a legacy-to-GCP data migration project:

Migration Planning & Assessment

Data Inventory & Audit: Identify and catalog all legacy data sources, including databases, data warehouses, files, and systems
Data Quality Assessment: Evaluate source data quality, identify gaps, inconsistencies, and anomalies
Migration Scope Definition: Help determine which data systems and datasets will be migrated vs. decommissioned
Impact Analysis: Document dependencies and identify systems/applications relying on legacy data

Data Mapping & Transformation

Schema Mapping: Map legacy database schemas to GCP target structures (BigQuery, Cloud SQL, etc.)
Data Transformation Logic: Define and document ETL/ELT rules and data transformation requirements
Data Lineage Documentation: Track data flow from source systems through transformation to GCP targets
Validation Rules: Create data quality checks and reconciliation processes

GCP Platform Support

GCP Architecture Familiarization: Learn GCP services relevant to migration (BigQuery, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, etc.)
Data Model Design: Collaborate on designing optimal data structures in GCP for performance and scalability
Tool & Script Development: Support creation of migration scripts and testing frameworks
Performance Optimization: Analyze and optimize query performance in BigQuery and other GCP services

Testing & Validation

Test Data Preparation: Create representative test datasets for UAT and validation
Migration Testing: Execute test migrations and validate data accuracy and completeness
Reconciliation: Compare source and target data to ensure successful migration with zero data loss
Performance Benchmarking: Track migration performance metrics and identify bottlenecks

Stakeholder Engagement

Requirements Gathering: Interview business users and data owners to understand data needs
Documentation: Create comprehensive migration runbooks and data dictionaries
Training Support: Help prepare documentation for post-migration data access and usage
Change Management: Communicate timelines, impacts, and new processes to stakeholders

Post-Migration Support

Data Validation: Perform comprehensive post-migration quality checks
Performance Monitoring: Monitor GCP resource utilization and query performance
Issue Resolution: Troubleshoot data discrepancies and resolve migration issues
Documentation Updates: Maintain accurate documentation of migrated data assets

Title: Senior Data Analyst (SQL, Power BI, Looker)
Description:
Job Title: Senior Data Analyst (SQL, Power BI, Looker)

Location: 100% Remote, Canada

Experience: 5+ Years

Rate: 62 to 65 CAD PH

Role Summary

NearSource is looking for a Senior Data Analyst to drive data-informed decision-making across large-scale platform initiatives. This role involves working with cross-functional teams to analyze complex datasets, deliver actionable insights, and support strategic business outcomes for a Fortune 500 product company.

Key Responsibilities


Collaborate with product managers and cross-functional teams to identify, define, and solve business problems using data-driven approaches.
Analyze large and complex datasets to extract actionable insights that influence product and business strategy.
Monitor and report on KPIs and OKRs, ensuring visibility into performance across platform initiatives.
Partner with product, engineering, and business stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver analytical solutions.
Design and deliver impactful dashboards and visualizations using Power BI, Looker, or similar tools.
Proactively identify trends and opportunities related to platform performance, user engagement, and business impact.
Develop advanced data models and apply statistical techniques to forecast trends and support decision-making.
Ensure data integrity through best practices in data validation, cleaning, and transformation.
Identify and resolve data discrepancies to maintain high-quality analytical outputs.



Must-Have Skills


Strong proficiency in SQL for data querying, transformation, and analysis.
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI and Looker.
Experience with data warehouse ELT tools, including Snowflake, Airflow, or dbt.
Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis and modeling.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure.
Solid understanding of statistical analysis and data modeling techniques.
Proven ability to analyze complex datasets and generate actionable insights.
Strong communication skills to present data insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.



Nice-to-Have Skills


Experience working in a SaaS environment or platform engineering organization.
Exposure to large-scale product analytics and user behavior analysis.
Familiarity with enterprise product ecosystems in the software industry.



Certifications


Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.
Master's degree in a quantitative field is an asset.



Apply now, or share your resume with salary expectations at careers@nearsource.ca.

Thank you for considering a career with us! Once you submit your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your resume thoroughly. If there's a strong match, we'll reach out to discuss your experience, role details, benefits, compensation, and next steps. While we strive for transparency, we may not be able to respond to every applicant due to high volume, but we genuinely appreciate your time and interest.

About NearSource

NearSource Technologies is a trusted partner for future-ready software consulting, enabling Fortune 500 enterprises to accelerate digital transformation. Our global engineering teams build and deploy impactful technology for some of the world's most admired brands, working directly on long-term client initiatives.

Equal Opportunity Statement

NearSource is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive and respectful environment. We celebrate diversity and do not discriminate based on race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability, or background. Innovation thrives when everyone feels empowered to contribute.

Title: Junior Business Advisor, Data & Analytics
Description:
The salary range for this position is CAD $35.37/Hr. - CAD $50.85/Hr.

Come work as a Junior Business Advisor, Data & Analytics with Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)!

Vancouver Coastal Health is looking for a Junior Business Advisor, Data & Analytics to join the CST Project Data Analytics Team. Apply today to join our team!

As a Junior Business Advisor, Data & Analytics with Vancouver Coastal Health you will


Occupy a foundational position within Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH), designed for those at the entry-level of their career in data analysis.
Be central to organizational data-driven decision-making processes, focus on the collection, process, and analysis of data to unearth valuable insights that inform strategic decisions for managers/leaders.
Collaborate with senior advisors/analysts and other team members to maintain data quality, work on creating and maintaining reports, assist with data analysis, creation of presentations and help enhance data-based procedures.
Utilize a strong analytical mindset and motivation to develop skills in data analysis and support organizational leadership within a collaborative team environment.



This work is framed within VCH’s commitments to our pillars of Indigenous Cultural Safety, Anti-Racism, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and Planetary Health.

Education & Experience


Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Health Administration, Business, Data Science, Statistics, Health Informatics, or a related discipline, supplemented with Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work with complex healthcare data.
Proficiency in data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, and statistical software (e.g., R, Python) required.



Knowledge & Abilities


Demonstrated familiarity with mean, median, variance, standard deviation, correlation, regression, and hypothesis testing can be fundamental.
Demonstrated ability to query databases using SQL is essential for extracting data.
Demonstrated knowledge of database systems like MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, or MS SQL.
Demonstrated ability to think critically and pay attention to details.
Fundamental knowledge of healthcare systems, terms, and procedures.
Outstanding communication skills to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
Strong team player with the ability to collaborate across departments.
Skilled at using analytical tools such as Excel, Access, Word and SPSS on a desktop.
Proven ability to manage and prioritize tasks.
Proven ability to interact well with others.
Ability to work on your own with minimal supervision.
Motivated to learn and develop skills, showing initiative in taking on new challenges and responsibilities.
Develops and maintains strong relationships with key internal and external partners to promote and enhance services provided with sensitivity to diversity and indigenous lens.
Practices the recognition, inclusion and support of all types and aspects of diversity at all stages of engagement, with an understanding of the issues faced by people from equity-deserving groups.
Ability to work independently with limited supervision.
Physical ability to perform the duties of the position.



The hours of work including days off and work area may be subject to change consistent with operational requirements and the provision of the Collective Agreement and applicable statutes. Successful applicants may be required to complete a Criminal Records Review Check.

As per Ministry of Health policy, all health care workers working in publicly-funded health care facilities are required to report their past receipt of certain vaccines or history of certain infections. Collecting these records will allow for offering of any missing vaccines, and for appropriate actions to be taken in the event of any future exposure to a communicable disease or during outbreaks. For all new hires and appointments to Vancouver Coastal Health, you will be asked to provide this information as part of the onboarding process.

Why Join Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)

VCH is a world class innovator in medical care, research and teaching, delivering service to more than one million BC residents. At VCH, we embrace thinking boldly, taking smart risks, and 'going first' when we believe it will lead to the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. We invite you to join us in creating healthy lives in healthy communities by showcasing our passion for care, connection to the communities we serve and our culture of teamwork that makes VCH a great place to work.


Comprehensive health benefits package, including MSP, extended health and dental and municipal pension plan
Grow your career with employer-paid training and leadership development opportunities
Wellness supports, including counselling, critical incident and innovative wellness services are available to employees and their immediate families
Award-winning recognition programs to honour staff, medical staff and volunteers
Access to exclusive discount offers and deals for VCH staff



At Vancouver Coastal Health, our vision of healthy lives in healthy communities inspires us and our values and pillars guide us. We support equitable practices, diverse teams and inclusive environments, which are vital to creating welcoming spaces where everyone can bring their whole selves to work and feel supported. We encourage applications from equity-deserving communities, including Indigenous Peoples and members of racialized groups, people with disabilities and people of all gender identities and expressions and sexual orientation.

Vancouver Coastal Health is proud to be recognized as one of BC’s Top Employers, Canada’s Best Diversity Employers and Canada's Top Employers for Young People in 2025.

Only short-listed applicants will be contacted for this posting.

***Employees of VCH must apply online via the Internal Career Portal on CareerHub, you are currently viewing the External Career Portal. Refer to the https//my.vch.ca/working-here/job-postings site for instructions on how to view internal job postings and how to apply as an employee. Current VCH employees who apply to this posting using this external site will be considered as an external candidate. Seniority will not apply.***

Thank you for your interest in Vancouver Coastal Health.

For all new hires and appointments to Vancouver Coastal Health, you will be asked to provide this information as part of the onboarding process.

Why Join Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)

VCH is a world class innovator in medical care, research and teaching, delivering service to more than one million BC residents. At VCH, we embrace thinking boldly, taking smart risks, and 'going first' when we believe it will lead to the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. We invite you to join us in creating healthy lives in healthy communities by showcasing our passion for care, connection to the communities we serve and our culture of teamwork that makes VCH a great place to work.

  • Comprehensive health benefits package, including MSP, extended health and dental and municipal pension plan
  • Grow your career with employer-paid training and leadership development opportunities
  • Wellness supports, including counselling, critical incident and innovative wellness services are available to employees and their immediate families
  • Award-winning recognition programs to honour staff, medical staff and volunteers
  • Access to exclusive discount offers and deals for VCH staff


At Vancouver Coastal Health, our vision of healthy lives in healthy communities inspires us and our values and pillars guide us. We support equitable practices, diverse teams and inclusive environments, which are vital to creating welcoming spaces where everyone can bring their whole selves to work and feel supported. We encourage applications from equity-deserving communities, including Indigenous Peoples and members of racialized groups, people with disabilities and people of all gender identities and expressions and sexual orientation.

Vancouver Coastal Health is proud to be recognized as one of BC’s Top Employers, Canada’s Best Diversity Employers and Canada's Top Employers for Young People in 2025.

Only short-listed applicants will be contacted for this posting.

***Employees of VCH must apply online via the Internal Career Portal on CareerHub, you are currently viewing the External Career Portal. Refer to the https//my.vch.ca/working-here/job-postings site for instructions on how to view internal job postings and how to apply as an employee. Current VCH employees who apply to this posting using this external site will be considered as an external candidate. Seniority will not apply.***

Thank you for your interest in Vancouver Coastal Health.

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
Data Modeler

Toronto, ON - Hybrid (3 Days WFO)

Contract -12 months







Role expectation: Designs and governs enterprise insurance data models aligned with ACORD standards to enable consistent, scalable, and integrated data ecosystems.







Responsibilities

1. Design conceptual, logical, and physical data models aligned to ACORD Information and Data Models

2. Develop enterprise canonical data models for core insurance domains (Policy, Claims, Party, Billing)

3. Ensure data consistency, normalization, and scalability across systems

4. Map business requirements to ACORD-compliant data structures

5. Apply and interpret ACORD Reference Architecture components (Information Model, Data Model, Business Glossary)

6. Perform end-to-end data mapping (source → ACORD canonical → target systems)

7. Enable data integration and interoperability across legacy and modern insurance platforms

8. Support API, messaging, and data exchange design aligned to ACORD standards

9. Design data warehouse / data lake models using ACORD-aligned structures

10. Collaborate with business and technical teams to translate requirements into data models

11. Establish and enforce data standards, naming conventions, and metadata definitions

12. Contribute to data governance, data quality, and lineage frameworks

13. Maintain data models, dictionaries, and mapping documentation with full traceability

14. Support modernization initiatives by aligning legacy data models with ACORD framework

15. Perform model validation, impact analysis, and compliance checks against enterprise and regulatory standards
