Title: Data Analyst, Product Analytics
Description:
New Position: This position is open due to an existing vacancy to support our evolving business needs.

Summary

Millions of professionals rely on Thomson Reuters’ products every day, and we want data to drive every decision we make about them. We're hiring a Data Analyst to join our team during an exciting period of AI-driven change – working closely with a senior analyst to shape how product teams measure success and act on insights.

The Analyst will serve as the primary owner of product analytics platforms (including tools such as Pendo), functioning as an embedded partner within the product team. This role requires the ability to evaluate qualitative AI outputs alongside quantitative data, translating complex AI behaviors into actionable insights through model performance evaluation, response quality assessment, and measurement of user trust and adoption.

This position is ideally suited for an experienced product or business analyst with a strong data foundation, hands-on experience with AI/ML product analytics, and a nuanced understanding of the challenges involved in measuring AI effectiveness. Success in this role requires strong people skills and a proactive, curious approach, finding ways to plug into product and engineering rhythms, building trust quickly, and continuously asking the right questions to uncover insights.

About The Role

In this opportunity as a Data Analyst, Product Analytics, you will be responsible for:


Data-Driven Decision Making: Use product analytics/data to recognize patterns and trends to understand user behavior and identify improvement opportunities.
Measurement Frameworks: Proactively establish measurement frameworks/success metrics for new product launches/features and drive actionability through data-driven insights.
Tag Management & Instrumentation: Maintain and expand Pendo tagging across the product, ensuring features, pages, and user interactions are accurately tracked as the product evolves. Partner with Product and Engineering to audit existing tags, deprecate stale instrumentation, and instrument net-new features ahead of launch to ensure day-one data coverage.
AI-Powered Analytics:  Apply AI-native analytics approaches, using LLMs to analyze unstructured data, leveraging RAG-based retrieval concepts to synthesize information across knowledge bases (hands-on implementation not required), and using GenAI tools to automate reporting and surface insights, with clear human-in-the-loop review, QA, and validation practices to ensure accuracy, reliability, trust, and appropriate use.
Stakeholder Management: Build and maintain strong working relationships with peers, product managers, and senior leadership, acting as a collaborative partner to drive analytical insights across your portfolio. This role also includes dedicated time (~10%) for reporting product usage data to cross-functional stakeholders outside of the product team.
Reporting & Communication: Own recurring and ad-hoc reporting for your product portfolio, translating complex analyses into clear narratives tailored to the audience, from deep-dive reviews for product and engineering partners to executive-ready summaries for senior leadership. Ensure reports are accurate, timely, and consistent.
Pendo Guide Development (10% of role): Author and maintain in-app Pendo guides to support users' onboarding and feature adoption. Guide development is often iterative and detail-heavy, requiring patient trial and error to get targeting, styling, and conditional logic right across edge cases. We're looking for someone who embraces that process, methodically works through unknowns, and doesn't lose momentum when the tenth variation is the one that finally works. Working knowledge of HTML/CSS and basic JavaScript is beneficial for building guides that go beyond default templates.
Thought Leadership: Demonstrate thought leadership by proactively leveraging analyses to understand products, derive insights, run experiments, and be the contact point for any analytics needs for your portfolio.


About You

The ideal candidate for the Data Analyst - Product Analytics position possesses:


At least 3 years of relevant experience in product analytics, data science, or a related discipline.
Advanced proficiency in SQL and Python.
Advanced expertise in developing and maintaining dashboards and reports.
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex datasets and convert findings into actionable recommendations.
Familiarity with natural language processing techniques and large language models.
Demonstrated experience driving data-driven decision-making within product teams, going beyond report generation to deliver insights through data storytelling. Experience with analyses such as customer journey mapping, audience profiling/segmentation, cohort analysis, tag management, and A/B testing.
Hands-on experience with AI tools for analytics.
Ability to implement effective QA and governance practices for AI-assisted analytics.
Excellent interpersonal and stakeholder engagement skills, with the capacity to integrate seamlessly into existing product and engineering workflows, exercise influence without direct authority, and demonstrate persistent curiosity to uncover underlying trends in data.
Hands-on experience with product analytics platforms such as Pendo, Amplitude, Adobe Analytics, and Google Analytics.


What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


Our use of AI within the recruitment process Thomson Reuters utilizes Artificial Intelligence (AI) to support parts of our global recruitment process. Unless you opt-out, our AI system will assess the information provided by you and compare it to the requirements listed for the role, and present the result to our recruitment personnel for further review. The AI system acts as a supporting tool, but there is always a human making the decision if you will be considered for the role

Thomson Reuters complies with local laws that require upfront disclosure of the expected pay range for a position. The base compensation range varies across locations. For Ontario, Canada, the base compensation range for this role is $70,000 CAD - $120,000 CAD. Base pay is positioned within the range based on several factors including an individual’s knowledge, skills and experience with consideration given to internal equity. Base pay is one part of a comprehensive Total Reward program which also includes flexible and supportive benefits and other wellbeing programs. This role may also be eligible for an Annual Bonus based on a combination of enterprise and individual performance.

About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com

Title: Reference Data Analyst
Description:
Why join the CanDeal Team?




CanDeal Group is a leading provider of electronic marketplaces and data services for Canadian dollar fixed income securities and derivatives. CanDeal’s Markets Division provides access to a deep pool of liquidity for Canadian government, agency, provincial and corporate bonds, as well as money market instruments and interest rate swaps. CanDeal Data & Analytics (DNA) delivers data and analytics products and other services that support business, trading and technology needs for participants in the Canadian capital markets.

CanDeal Group is a growing and entrepreneurial organization with a solid foundation in the Canadian fixed income markets and an ownership group which includes BMO Nesbitt Burns Inc., CIBC World Markets Inc., National Bank Financial Inc., RBC Dominion Securities Inc., Scotia Capital Inc., TD Securities Inc. and TMX Group.

This is an exciting time to join a growing organization led by visionary leaders who are helping to shape their industry’s future.




Job Purpose

The successful candidate will form a key part of the Reference Data team responsible for building out a comprehensive best-in-class fixed income reference data solution. This role will require a deep understanding of all relevant Canadian fixed income reference data attributes covering each security type, optionality, factors, classifications and how this information is applied to fixed income pricing, analytics and integration into global vendor workflows. The role will also require full implementation of data governance, quality, validation, and workflow management practices on a real time basis.




Primary Duties

The candidate will perform a wide range of duties including but not limited to the following:

Establishing a comprehensive reference data library covering 150K Canadian fixed income securities.
Implementing data governance, validation, workflow and system check throughout the life cycle.
Ensure the reference data is fully up to date to ensure fixed income pricing and analytics have the most timely and accurate source of truth.
Operational maintenance of reference data for the Security Master products.
Develop and maintain strong relationships with syndicate desks to obtain issuance documents and resolve any missing information.
Identity and troubleshoot discrepancies in data capture. Document and communicate discrepancies to developers and follow through to resolution.
Manage client (internal and external) queries and client escalations.
Liaise with counterparts/stakeholders within the wider team.




Secondary Duties

User acceptance testing for new or enhanced product functionality. Execute test cases to confirm completion of technical, functional requirements for new fixed income product and system releases.
Identify data quality improvement projects and opportunities to enhance operational efficiency.




Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

5 years+ of deep experience building fixed income reference data solutions in a global data vendor
Deep understanding and experience building data governance, validations, workflow controls and downstream integration into pricing, analytics and risk systems
Broad understanding of global vendor use cases, dealers and client integrations
Strong investigating and problem-solving skills - acute attention to detail.
Demonstrates analytical thinking and sound judgement when making decisions.
Outstanding organizational skills, highly motivated and adaptable to a changing
business environment.
Must have exceptional time management skills.
Bilingualism (French/English) is an asset.




Key Qualifications

Long term desire to stay focused on reference data.
Knowledge of fixed income reference data (experience at the banks an asset – fixed income/syndicate desks).
Content on handling data entry and daily tasks (ex. Experience in handling Capital Market settlements).
Experience with Data Management and Governance.
Experience with SQL.




Education

University degree in a related discipline. (Business and/or Finance focus).




If you are interested in this opportunity, please send your resume to: Careers@candeal.com




Please inform us if you require any accommodation during the hiring process.

  • Content on handling data entry and daily tasks (ex. Experience in handling Capital Market settlements).
  • Experience with Data Management and Governance.
  • Experience with SQL.


Education

  • University degree in a related discipline. (Business and/or Finance focus).


If you are interested in this opportunity, please send your resume to: Careers@candeal.com


Please inform us if you require any accommodation during the hiring process.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
ID Logistics is a leading international contract logistics company with operations in more than 19 countries worldwide providing innovative supply chain solutions for some of the world’s largest retailers and manufacturers. As the first site in Canada, our focus is on operational excellence, safety, and customer satisfaction, with a strong commitment to teamwork, innovation, and continuous improvement. 




ID Logistics Canada – XYY1 in Ajax, ON is looking for an ICQA Data Analyst to join our growing team for three (3) months. Reporting to the Stock Manager the ICQA Data Analyst will be responsible for the following:




Responsibilities:




· Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets related to inventory accuracy, cycle counts, quality trends, and operational performance.
 · Develop daily, weekly, and monthly ICQA reports, dashboards, and KPIs for leadership review.
 · Identify discrepancies, patterns, and root causes impacting inventory accuracy and quality assurance.
 · Support cycle count processes by validating results and tracking variances.
 · Conduct deep-dive investigations into inventory issues, shrink, and process gaps.
 · Partner with Operations Supervisors, Inventory Control, and Quality teams to implement corrective actions.
 · Monitor key ICQA performance metrics such as accuracy %, defect rate, count compliance, and audit results.
 · Maintain data integrity through validation, reconciliation, and error-checking processes.
 · Present insights and findings to management to support data-driven decision making.
 · Support ongoing and ad-hoc ICQA projects during the contract term.




The above responsibilities describe the general nature and level of work being performed. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibility, duties and skills required for this position. Additional duties may be assigned as needed.




Qualifications:




Post-secondary education in Data Analytics, Supply Chain, Business, Mathematics, or related field.
 Proven experience in data analysis, preferably in a warehouse, logistics, 3PL, or fulfillment environment.
 Strong proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, formulas, Power Query an asset).
 Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
 Knowledge of warehouse management systems (WMS) or inventory software is an asset.
 Understanding of ICQA principles, cycle counts, root-cause analysis, and inventory workflows.
 Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving abilities.
 Ability to communicate complex data insights clearly and concisely.
 Ability to work independently and meet deadlines within a fast-paced environment.




Accessibility & Transparency at ID Logistics Canada
 At ID Logistics Canada, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace. We welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and will provide accommodations throughout the recruitment, selection, and employment process upon request. If you require accommodation at any stage, please contact us and we will work with you to meet your needs.




In alignment with Ontario’s Pay Transparency and Employment Standards, ID Logistics Canada provides clear information on compensation and working conditions. We do not require Canadian work experience as a condition of employment and are dedicated to fair, transparent, and respectful hiring practices. Applicants will be informed of the outcome of their application within the required timelines, and all postings and related materials are retained in accordance with provincial legislation.

Title: Customer Insights Analyst, Loblaws
Description:
Overview

The Customer Insights Analyst, Loblaws, 1PEP reports to the Sr. Customer Insights Manager for Loblaws (LCL). This position acts as a category analyst with Loblaws for PepsiCo Food & Beverage categories. This person will translate business data to compelling insights, identify opportunities and develop fact-based recommendations that support achievement of both the retailer and PepsiCo sales and profit objectives. The Customer Insights Analyst is responsible for providing on-going assessment and monitoring to determine the effectiveness of various business tactics.

What you can expect from us:


Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits designed to fit your unique needs
A dynamic and inclusive culture that promotes you to bring your whole-self to work
Inspiring positive change for people and the planet with sustainability goals
A supportive team that will encourage your professional growth and development


Total Compensation: $68,900 - 112,160

Responsibilities


Customer Engagement: Establish strong working relationships to increase depth of understanding and build trust with retail partners via regular touch points
Category Insights: Analyze business trends and segment performance to identify opportunities to drive category sales and profit
Growth Drivers: Evaluate assortment, shelving, pricing and promotion to influence the retailer execution strategy and/or JBP/CDA process
Space Evaluation: Examine shelf space measures and propose a shopper friendly planogram, adhering to merchandising standards
Presentations: Synthesize category, consumer and shopper insights to deliver a clear and concise story leading to fact-based and actionable recommendations
Capabilities: Leverage a solid understanding of the analytical process and tools to support the successful achievement of customer insights and sales agenda


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree
2+ years relevant CPG experience, with an understanding of grocery retail merchandising
Strong ability to pull disparate pieces of data together for a cohesive and insightful category story
Strong presentation and storytelling skills
High proficiency in the use of Excel, Power Point
Experience with Nielsen/POS data and Space Management software (e.g. JDA/Spaceman/Apollo/ProSpace) is an asset


Careers To Smile About:

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

PepsiCo may use Artificial Intelligence as part of its hiring process. Specifically, we may use AI-based tools to screen, assess, or select applicants.

This posting is an existing vacancy.

Title: Intern, Data Science (Fall 2026)
Description:
Build something people love

Wealthsimple's mission is to help everyone achieve financial freedom – by making financial services simple, transparent, and low-cost. We're Canada's largest fintech, trusted by over 3 million clients with more than $100 billion in assets. We move fast, we own our work, and we care deeply about the people using our products. If that sounds like you, keep reading.

We're proud of what we've built — and we're just getting started. Read our Culture Manual and learn more about how we work.

Internships @ Wealthsimple

At Wealthsimple, we offer 4 to 8-month internships that are open to co-op and non-co-op students, and recent grads. During your internship, you will have the opportunity to contribute to projects that are changing the landscape of financial services for Canadians. You will be on a team that supports your growth, provides mentorship, and connects you to the broader Wealthsimple community!

Our internship program follows a hybrid work model, where you’ll be working from our Toronto headquarters on Wednesdays and Thursdays. This structure offers the flexibility of remote work, while also providing the collaboration, connection, and mentorship that come from being together in person.

Eligibility

✔ Currently enrolled as a student at a Canadian post-secondary institution or technical bootcamp

✔ New graduates - Within 6 months of your graduation date

✔ Available to work full-time hours

✔ Residing in Canada

✔ Able to commute to our Toronto HQ on Wednesdays and Thursdays

✔ At least 2 paid work terms completed

✔ Legally authorized to work in Canada (Wealthsimple does not provide immigration or relocation support for interns)

Application Deadline: May 19th, 2026 9:00AM ET

Data @ Wealthsimple

Data Science at Wealthsimple turns complex, real-world financial data into decisions, products, and experiences that help millions build wealth. We partner closely with Product, Engineering, and business leaders to design experiments, build models, and surface insights that shape what we ship — from client-facing features to growth strategies and risk policies. We give teams the insights, tools, and decision frameworks they need to ship impactful work — ensuring we’re building data-informed products our clients trust and love.

About The Teams

Risk & Financial Decision Science

Helps Wealthsimple grow responsibly by combining experimentation, modeling, and domain expertise to shape credit, fraud, and financial risk policies. This team turns complex, real-world data into clear recommendations — from detecting suspicious activity to understanding how economic conditions affect our products. If you're passionate about keeping a fast-moving fintech both safe and scalable, this is your team.

Product Decision Science

Partners closely with Product, Engineering, and Design to decide what we build, how we launch it, and how we measure success. This team designs experiments, defines metrics, and builds data products that explain how clients actually use Wealthsimple — validating ideas, iterating quickly, and doubling down on what works. If you're passionate about using data to tell clear stories and ship better product experiences, this is your team.

Growth Decision Science

Focuses on how Wealthsimple grows — from acquisition and onboarding to engagement, retention, and cross-sell across our product suite. This team builds models and insights that power personalization, campaign strategy, and lifecycle optimization to grow net deposits sustainably. If you're passionate about finding leverage in funnels, experiments, and growth loops — and seeing your work show up in real business outcomes — this is your team.

Who We're Looking For


Take ownership without being asked — they see something broken and fix it
Ship fast and know what to cut
Learn on the go and ask for help when they need it
Use AI tools in their workflow and are curious about what else is possible
Care about the person on the other end of what they're building
Believe that debate, inclusivity, and transparency make for better products


Our Tech Stack


Python-first environment with Pandas, scikit-learn, NumPy, and Jupyter
SQL for data access and manipulation across large, production datasets
Statistical foundations across both frequentist and Bayesian approaches
Machine learning toolkit spanning regression, decision trees, and beyond
Experimentation infrastructure for A/B testing and causal inference at scale


The Interview Process

Applications close on May 19th, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

Step 1 — Application + Video Submission: Apply and submit a short video response with a written transcript. Applications open May 4–17.

Step 2 — (May 19th-May 29th) Technical Interview: A 1-hour interview in Python with a technical interviewer.

Step 3 — (June 1st-June 12th) - Project Review Interview: A 30-minute conversation with one of our engineering managers about how you work and what drives you.

Step 4 — Offers start to be extended the week of June 8th

Why Wealthsimple?

🤑 Competitive salary with group savings matching plan using Wealthsimple for Business

🌴 Generous vacation days, wellness days and unlimited sick & mental health days

🎉 Intern programming including educational workshops, hackathons, and mentorship

🌎 A wide variety of peer and company-led Employee Resource Groups (ie. Rainbow, Inclusive Gender Network, Black @ WS)

What past interns have said about their experience at Wealthsimple:

"I absolutely loved the term here and had a great chance to learn new things and meet many amazingly talented people! I also really appreciated the mentorship program, having someone who was an alumni from my school+program was a great chance to learn more and help plan my career and academic journey!"

"A lot of autonomy and trust from teammates; In-person office experience allowed for a lot of deeper friendships; I genuinely like the Wealthsimple product which made it very exciting to work on it"

"The other interns were really a highlight as well as all of the things that y'all planned for us. I also would say my own team really made me feel at home and welcomed. I love the codebase and engineering tools a lot and will miss it!"

With over 1,000 employees coast to coast in North America, be a part of our Canadian success story and help shape the financial future of millions — join us!

Why Wealthsimple?

🌸 Top-tier health benefits and life insurance

📈 Long-term group savings with employer match, through Wealthsimple for Business

🌴 20 vacation days, 4 wellness days, and unlimited sick and mental health days per year

✈️ 90 days away: work outside Canada for up to 90 days per year

👥 Employee resource groups, including Rainbow (2SLGBTQ), Women of WS, and Black at WS

🌎 We are a hybrid team with over 1,500 employees across North America. The people are one of the best parts of working here: you'll collaborate with incredibly talented, curious, and driven teammates who are deeply committed to doing great work.

ICYMI

Technology & Innovation at Wealthsimple: We move quickly and build thoughtfully. That means we're always looking for better ways to work — whether that's new tools, AI, or rethinking how we approach a problem. We don't expect you to have all the answers, but we do expect curiosity and a willingness to evolve alongside the products we're building.

Inclusion Statement: We're building products for a diverse world, and we need a diverse team to do it well. We strongly encourage applications from everyone, regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status.

Accessibility Statement: We're committed to an accessible hiring experience. If you need any accommodations throughout the interview process, please let us know — we'll work with you to make sure you have what you need. We also welcome any feedback on how we can better accommodate candidates with accessibility needs.

AI in Hiring: We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of our hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our team but don't replace human judgment – all final hiring decisions are made by people. If you have questions about how your data is used, reach out to us.

Compensation Range: CA$74K - CA$90K

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Power BI / Looker)
Description:
Job Title: Senior Data Analyst (Power BI / Looker)

Job Location: Toronto, Ontario, Canada

Work Mode: Hybrid (Must come to office at least once a month)

Working Hours: 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM

Bill Rate: 55 to 65 CAD per hour

Role Summary

We are hiring for this role, where the selected candidate will work on a project focused on data-driven decision-making and analytics. You’ll help transform complex datasets into meaningful insights while collaborating with cross-functional teams to support business goals.

Required Experience and Skills:

Minimum 5 years of experience in a Data Analyst role
Strong experience with Python, PySpark, and Snowflake SQL
Hands-on experience with Power BI and Looker
Strong data analysis and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills, including requirement gathering and stakeholder management
Strong understanding of databases and ability to write PySpark or native SQL
Experience working with systems like Hive, Snowflake, and Presto
Strong skills in Excel
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment

Bonus Skills:

Experience with supervised and unsupervised learning techniques
Experience debugging and analyzing data using Spark/PySpark
Familiarity with Airflow
Experience working in a cross-functional environment

Interview Process:

Data Analytics Round, including SQL/Python coding test (60 minutes)
Hiring Manager Round focused on experience and team fit (60 minutes)
Power BI Round, including hands-on problem solving and conceptual questions (60 minutes)
Final Round with Product Manager (30 minutes)

Minimum Education Required:

Bachelor’s degree or higher in a quantitative field

Interested in this opportunity? Apply now or share your resume at hr@techedinlabs.com and our recruitment team will review your profile and reach out if it aligns with client requirements.

We value every application and aim to respond to those closely matching the role criteria. Even if you don’t hear back this time, we encourage you to stay connected with us for future opportunities.

About Techedin:

Techedin is a global IT staffing and technology services firm helping enterprises build high-performance tech teams. We support innovation at scale by connecting skilled professionals to leading companies and impactful projects. We prioritize transparency, responsiveness, and the right fit.

Equal Opportunity Statement: At Techedin, we welcome applicants of all backgrounds, communities, and experiences. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion drives us to create hiring experiences that are open, fair, and human-first.

Title: Analyst, Digital Data, Global Wealth Management
Description:
Requisition ID: 260629

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The role of the Analyst, Digital Data, Global Wealth Management (GWM) involves embedding advanced analytics capabilities into the Bank’s wealth digital platforms. You will develop and operationalize data strategies that drive client engagement, improve customer experience, and business growth across GWM channels. You will partner closely with GWM product, digital, advisor platform, and analytics teams to translate data into actionable insights that improve client outcomes, advisor workflows, and strategic decision making.

Is this role right for you? In this role you will:


Partner with Global Wealth Management product, digital, and advisor platform teams to enable data driven decision making
Define and execute analytics strategy and roadmap aligned to GWM digital analytics priorities, including digital footprint across all platforms
Measure and analyze performance of wealth digital experiences (e.g., advisor portals, client-facing digital platforms, onboarding and servicing flows)
Identify opportunities to improve client experience and advice quality and increase advisor effectiveness and adoption of digital tools
Deliver analytics solutions and data products that support client segmentation and personalization, advisor insights and decision support and lifecycle and journey optimization
Collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, and platform teams to automate trusted data pipelines and reporting, implement data quality, controls, and monitoring, and ensure analytics solutions are scalable and compliant
Communicate insights clearly and effectively to Wealth business leaders, digital partners, and senior stakeholders, translating complex analyses into actionable recommendations
Support experimentation (A/B testing, pilots, proofs of value) to validate hypotheses and measure business impact in regulated wealth environments


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? – We’d love to work with you if you have:


A University degree in Business, Finance, or a relevant STEM discipline (Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics) or equivalent practical experience
Hands-on experience with data analysis, performance measurement, and stakeholder engagement
A strong ability to simplify and communicate complex analytical insights to business and executive audiences
Experience or strong familiarity with Wealth Management concepts, such as product and client journeys, products and client segmentation and regulatory and compliance considerations is an asset
A proven ability to clean, transform, and analyze large datasets across multiple formats (e.g., structured, semi-structured, logs, JSON, flat files)
Proficiency in SQL and at least one analytical/programming language (Python or R preferred)
Working knowledge of data science and analytics libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, scikit learn, Git)
Experience working with big data or cloud-based analytics platforms and building large, performant queries
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Qlik
A familiarity with digital analytics or behavioral data tools (e.g., Adobe Analytics, Google Analytics, Splunk) applied to digital wealth platforms
A foundational understanding of analytical techniques including descriptive and diagnostic analytics, predictive modeling and segmentation and time series and trend analysis
Strong project management and collaboration skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
The ability to manage multiple initiatives and priorities in parallel
Strong attention to detail and data accuracy
A comfort working both independently and collaboratively
An analytical, structured, and solution oriented mindset


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Senior Analyst, Analytics & Insights
Description:
The Klick Group—comprising Klick Health (including Klick Katalyst and btwelve), Klick Media Group, Klick Applied Sciences (including Klick Labs), Klick Consulting, and Klick Ventures—is an ecosystem dedicated to realizing the full potential of our people and clients in life sciences. Founded in 1997, we have offices in New York, Philadelphia, Saratoga Springs, Toronto, London, São Paulo, and Singapore. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.

About Our Analytics Craft

Our Analytics craft helps clients use data to understand audiences, optimize engagement, and support meaningful business decisions. The team works across digital measurement, performance analysis, optimization, and predictive modelling, partnering closely with media, strategy, technology, and client services to deliver insights grounded in rigorous analysis and clear communication.

Job Description

This role contributes hands-on analysis and clear storytelling that help senior stakeholders understand performance and guide business decisions. The Senior Analyst translates complex datasets into actionable insights, partners with technical teams to ensure data quality, and supports measurement across digital channels. The role blends analytical rigor, curiosity, and collaboration to support clients across a variety of data-driven needs.

What You’ll Do:


Perform hands-on analysis and deliver insights for senior-level audiences
Define data requirements and collaborate with database specialists to support business questions
Support data collection and measurement across digital channels
Communicate findings clearly to internal and client stakeholders
Apply quantitative techniques to help improve performance and decision-making


Qualifications

Required:


5+ years of hands-on data analysis experience with large, multi-dimensional datasets
3+ years working with web analytics platforms, including Google Analytics
Familiarity with tag management systems such as Google Tag Manager, Tealium, or Ensighten
Experience analyzing digital marketing channels such as media, CRM, or mobile apps
Knowledge of statistical analysis principles and methods
Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and analysis


Desired:


Experience working with WebTrends or SiteCatalyst
Ability to connect analytical findings to business outcomes in client-facing settings
Experience with predictive modelling or marketing optimization approaches
Strong communication skills for explaining analytical concepts to non-technical audiences
Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data and measurement needs
Curiosity for new analytical methods, tools, and emerging digital-measurement approaches


Additional Information

This role is backfilling an existing vacancy at Klick. The base salary range for this position is between CAD $85,000 - $105,000. The final amount offered will be determined based on several factors such as a candidate's work location, their unique skill set, education, and prior work experience. This posting is for an existing vacancy at Klick.

Our Commitment to Inclusion

Klick is consciously creating a culture where everyone can thrive and grow in their careers. We believe that our best work comes from our diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented communities to apply, including Black people, Indigenous peoples, and other people of color, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities and people with intersectional identities. We're also committed to developing an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment process and work environment. Should you require any accommodation, please contact us at careers@klick.com and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs and ensure you have a positive experience.

Title: Student, Data Analytics / Data Science (Fall 2026)
Description:
Our student and new graduate programs offer a chance to explore Sun Life from the inside. You’ll make a difference in the lives of our customers and communities, while building the skills and experience that allow you to grow in a meaningful and rewarding way.

Job Description

Our student and new graduate programs offer a chance to explore Sun Life from the inside. You’ll make a difference in the lives of our customers and communities, while building the skills and experience that allow you to grow in a meaningful and rewarding way.

Term: September 8th 2026 to December 18th 2026 (4 months)

The advanced analytics and business intelligence team supports the RA&S business unit with actional data, insights, and models to help drive the business forward. It is a dynamic team full of seasoned data professionals working to help our advisors and our clients thrive.

What will you do?


Work with stakeholders to draw business insights from analytical outputs
Communicate recommendations and data insights to leadership teams
Help deliver automated reporting on key performance metrics (KPIs) using Tableau
Work with key datasets to help form proactive insights


What do you need to succeed?


This opportunity is available to students with a graduation date of December 2026 or later
Be enrolled in an undergraduate program in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering or equivalent
Have an understanding of data analysis, data management, data modelling
Proficiency in SQL would be an asset
Proficiency in Python would be an asset


What is in it for you?


A friendly, collaborative, and inclusive culture where you’re an innovator and trusted partner
An environment of continuous learning and improvement
We’re honoured to be recognized as a 2022 Best Workplaces in Canada by Great Place to Work® Canada
The ability to learn cutting edge data and analytics skills that will help jumpstart your career


The Base Pay range is for the primary location for which the job is posted for. The Base Pay which you will be considered for will vary depending on the Campus program you are selected for.

Eligible Sun Life employees for various Campus Graduate programs will also participate in various incentive plans, payment under which is discretionary and subject to individual and company performance

Diversity and inclusion have always been at the core of our values at Sun Life. A diverse workforce with wide perspectives and creative ideas benefits our Clients, the communities where we operate and all of us as colleagues. We welcome applications from qualified individuals from all backgrounds.

Persons with disabilities who need accommodation in the application process, or those needing job postings in an alternative format, may e-mail a request to thebrightside@sunlife.com.

We are proud to be a hybrid organization that offers our employees the choice and flexibility to work from both the office and virtually based on the needs of the business, our Clients and you! Several flexible work options are available and can be discussed throughout the selection process depending on the role requirements and individual needs.

We may use artificial intelligence to support candidate sourcing, screening, interview scheduling.

We thank all applicants for showing an interest in this position. Only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Job Category

Temporary Employee

Posting End Date

22/05/2026

Title: Analytics Lead, LUS
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Data and analytics are at the heart of Lyft's products and decision-making. As a member of the Lyft Urban Solutions team, you will play a key role in shaping the future of bikeshare by leveraging data to improve the performance of our bikeshare and scooter markets across America. A successful candidate thrives in a dynamic and collaborative environment, has a natural curiosity, and isn’t afraid to dive deep.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with Operations, Policy, Engineering, Product and Finance teams to drive data-informed strategies that align our operations and products with city transportation goals and user needs. You will work in a fast-paced environment where analytical insights directly impact decisions ranging from fleet management and operations performance to pricing and product features to long-term investments in bikesharing infrastructure.

We’re looking for a passionate and driven Data Analyst to tackle some of the most complex and impactful challenges in micromobility. If you’re excited about shaping the future of urban mobility through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Responsibilities:


Partner with Product, Engineering, Policy, Operations, Finance and other cross-functional stakeholders on initiatives to conduct deep-dive analyses to root cause issues and propose solutions
Develop frameworks, business logic and scalable processes to streamline reporting and drive decision-making
Forecast operational requirements needed to maintain high service levels and meet contractual and financial targets
Work closely with cross-functional partners to deliver data quickly, reliably and accurately to our city partners
Monitor and diagnose KPI performance and present findings to senior leadership


Experience:


3-5+ years experience in data analytics in a high-growth environment, preferably a consulting, operations or transportation / logistics space
Bachelor's Degree or equivalent relevant professional experience
Highly skilled in SQL and quantitative analysis, you can deep dive into large amounts of data, draw meaningful insights, dissect business issues and draw actionable conclusions
Ability to develop scalable approaches and produce data visualizations to drive business insights and provide tangible solutions; experience building dashboards for performance analysis is a plus
Extreme comfort working with ambiguity. Ability to translate unclear issues or unstructured problems into clearly defined requirements with minimal oversight
Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships, trust and influence with cross-functional partners
Great communication (listening, written, and oral) skills with the ability to present findings & recommendations targeted to the audience in question
Strong attention to detail, structured thinking and experiences developing processes to reduce human error
Adept at contextualizing real world operations into analytical problem solving
A strong sense of product ownership - you’re constantly looking for ways to improve the customer’s experience and aren’t afraid to get your hands dirty to do so
Passionate about sustainable mobility and active transportation
Bonus: Proficiency in Python and associated data science libraries


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits


Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $96,000 - CAD $120,000, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

This job fills an existing vacancy.

Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $96,000 - CAD $120,000, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

Title: Associate, Measurement & Reporting Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

This Investor Services role focuses on legacy reporting migration within RBC's Data and Digital center. The team builds high-performing, resilient, scalable data and BI solutions for internal and external stakeholders. You'll join an agile team as a Business Intelligence & Analytics professional, leveraging various technologies to deliver innovative solutions. The position emphasizes continuous growth, analytical thinking, and collaborative problem-solving to strengthen RBC's data capabilities and digital transformation initiatives.

What will you do?


Requirements Gathering – Elicit, analyze, and document business requirements, rules, and user stories; collaborate with business, product, and development teams throughout delivery.
Project Coordination – Serve as key project team member ensuring timely, on-budget delivery alongside delivery leads, developers, and data experts.
Stakeholder Communication – Proactively engage with internal stakeholders (Operations, Client Success, Data Engineering) to analyze requirements and deliver necessary documentation.
Primary Contact – Act as first-line liaison for internal stakeholders across multiple departments.
Data Integrity – Review and validate data quality for accurate analysis, business insights, and hypothesis testing.
BI & Reporting – Build Power BI reports/dashboards and translate findings into executive-level communications.
Analytics Expertise – Analyze data trends, interpret results, develop KPIs, explain drivers, and communicate insights clearly.
Technical Skills – Demonstrate proficiency in relational databases, OLAP, Kimball methodologies, ETL processes, data modeling, and Power BI visualization.


What do you need to succeed?

Must haves:


1-3 years of experience in business intelligence and analytics field with strong understanding of technical aspects while working with the business on value driven analysis
Strong business communication and stakeholder management skills
Advanced problem-solving skills
Proficiency in Microsoft PowerBI, Report Builder, Excel and PowerPoint


Nice-to-have:


Strong knowledge of fundamental data concepts (for example, but not limited to groups, hierarchies, data structures).
Broad knowledge of financial products, processes, and systems
Strong aptitude to learn and improve knowledge.


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable.
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact.
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team.
Flexible work/life balance options.
Opportunities to do challenging work.
Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities.
Access to a variety of job opportunities across business.

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Data Mining, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Quantitative Research, Research Documents

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Proofpoint is a global leader in human- and agent-centric cybersecurity. We protect how people, data, and AI agents connect across email, cloud, and collaboration tools. Over 80 of the Fortune 100, 10,000 large enterprises, and millions of smaller organizations trust Proofpoint to stop threats, prevent data loss, and build resilience across their people and AI workflows. Our mission is simple safeguard the digital world and empower people to work securely and confidently. Join us in our pursuit to defend data and protect people.

How We Work

At Proofpoint you’ll be part of a global team that breaks barriers to redefine cybersecurity guided by our BRAVE core values

Bold in how we dream and innovate

Responsive to feedback, challenges and opportunities

Accountable for results and best in class outcomes

Visionary in future focused problem-solving

Exceptional in execution and impact

Job Summary

The People Data Analyst will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting people and talent acquisition data to support business strategy and HR initiatives. This role will assist in designing and developing dashboards, delivering reports to various business functions, and contributing to HR projects. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in data analysis, excellent communication skills, and an interest in people analytics.


Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship now or in the future.
This role requires on-site presence at one of our offices located in Sunnyvale, CA;Pittsburgh, PA; Draper, UT; Toronto, CAN; Argentina


Key Responsibilities


Data Analysis & Strategy Analyze people data to identify trends, insights, and opportunities. Use creative problem-solving to inform business strategy and HR initiatives.
Dashboard Design & Development Create and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI to present data insights clearly and effectively.
Cross-Functional Collaboration Partner with HR, Talent Acquisition, and other business units to understand data needs and provide actionable insights.
Process Documentation Outline, document, and refine processes related to data analytics, including data sharing, usage, and related use cases.
HR Project Contribution Support HR projects such as Employee Listening, Performance Management, and Workforce Planning by providing data-driven insights and analysis.
Data Architecture & Compliance Implement improvements to data architecture and ensure compliance with internal policies and external regulations regarding data collection and sharing.
Training & Best Practices Lead training sessions for users on best practices for utilizing people data and data visualization tools to enhance data-driven decision-making.
Ad Hoc Data Requests Manage ad-hoc data requests from various stakeholders and develop self-service solutions to improve efficiency and accessibility.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Human Resources, Business Administration, Statistics, or a related field.
Proven experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or PowerBI.
Advanced proficiency in Excel, ability to create complex formulas, pivot-tables, lookups, etc.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and present findings in a clear and actionable manner.
Familiarity with people and talent acquisition data, including understanding HR metrics and KPIs.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to partner effectively with various business functions.
Experience in outlining and documenting data processes and use cases.
Understanding of data compliance and regulatory requirements related to data collection and sharing.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and respond to ad hoc data requests efficiently.
Knowledge and experience with SQL is a plus.


Why Proofpoint?

At Proofpoint, we believe that an exceptional career experience includes a comprehensive compensation and benefits package. Here are just a few reasons you’ll love working with us


Competitive compensation
Comprehensive benefits
Career success on your terms
Flexible work environment
Annual wellness and community outreach days
Always on recognition for your contributions
Global collaboration and networking opportunities


Our Culture

Our culture is rooted in values that inspire belonging, empower purpose and drive success-every day, for everyone.

We encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. If you need accommodation during the application or interview process, please reach out to accessibility@proofpoint.com.

How To Apply

Interested? Submit your application along with any supporting information- we can’t wait to hear from you!

Consistent with Proofpoint values and applicable law, we provide the following information to promote pay transparency and equity. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets as set out below. Pay within these ranges varies and depends on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. The actual offer will be based on the individual candidate. The range provided may represent a candidate range and may not reflect the full range for an individual tenured employee. This role may be eligible for variable compensation and/or equity. We offer a competitive benefits package, including flexible time off, a comprehensive well-being program with two paid Wellbeing Days and two paid Volunteer Days per year, plus a three-week Work from Anywhere option.

Base Pay Ranges

SF Bay Area, New York City Metro Area

Base Pay Range 92,800.00 - 136,070.00 USD

California (excludes SF Bay Area), Colorado, Connecticut, Illinois, Washington DC Metro, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, Texas, Washington, Virginia, and Alaska

Base Pay Range 75,000.00 - 110,000.00 USD

All other cities and states excluding those listed above

Base Pay Range 68,600.00 - 100,650.00 USD

Title: Business Data Analyst – SQL/ Data Warehousing (Banking)
Company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
Description:
Role: Business Data Analyst – SQL/ Data Warehousing (Banking)


MUST HAVE Skills:





• Strong knowledge of PL SQL / T-SQL
• Experience in Data Modelling (any designer tool), Gap analysis, and MS Visio
• Good understanding of Data Warehousing concepts and ETL processes
• Experience in driving requirement sessions/meetings with stakeholders
• Strong knowledge of SQL for data analysis and data profiling across legacy systems





NICE TO HAVE Skills:





• Knowledge of Banking domain (accounts, customer data, transaction data)
• Experience with Sybase PowerDesigner for data modeling
• Knowledge of Cloud technologies (AWS preferred)





Key Responsibilities: Analyze Legacy System





• Analyze mapping documents of target tables to determine operational objectives by studying business functions, gathering information, and evaluating output requirements
• Track target field mapping back to origin data elements in source systems
• Perform data analysis and profiling across different legacy systems using SQL
• Document source-to-target data flow including:





Business transformation rules

Key cutting / surrogate key logic

Reference data usage for enrichment
• Extract business rules from code when mapping documents are not available


L2 / L3 Model Responsibilities:





• Support creation of L2 model with Business Analysts, Solution Architects, and stakeholders
• Collaborate with data scientists, business analysts, and IT teams to integrate L2/L3 models into pipelines





Mapping Design of New L2/L3:





• Prepare technical design and data movement mapping for L2 model
• Review and validate key cutting process with L2 schema owners
• Ensure BORT system data is loaded into L2 schema
• Define L2 to L3 transformation rules
• Implement custom surrogate key processes on L3 tables
• Handle one-off system data loads into L3 schema
• Collaborate on data governance and data quality frameworks
• Ensure compliance with industry standards and reduce data security risks

General Responsibilities:








• Define operational objectives by studying business functions and requirements
• Design and analyze requirements and system modifications
• Define project requirements, milestones, and deliverables
• Track project progress and publish status reports
• Maintain mapping documentation for users
• Ensure confidentiality of operational data
• Participate in workshops and continuous learning
• Support intake requests for new projects and changes
• Work closely with business and technology teams for data needs
• Prepare technical design documentation as per standards

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
Digital Analyst

Client: Retail Client

Role: Digital Analyst

Job Type: Contract (7 months, possible extension)

Location: Hybrid - 1 day/week – Downtown Toronto, ON

Pay Rate - $50/hr – $75/hr




Your New Company

Our client, a very well-known Retail company hiring a Digital Analyst for a contract opportunity.




Your New Role:

Analyze Product Performance: Dive deep into product usage data to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement across our digital platforms.
Customer Insights: Conduct in-depth analysis of customer behaviour, focusing on customer retention, churn analysis, and customer journey analytics to understand engagement patterns and pain points.
Data Extraction & Manipulation: Utilize SQL and Python extensively for data extraction, transformation, and analysis from various sources.
Reporting & Visualization: Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-serve analytics tools using BI platforms (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI) to monitor key product metrics and communicate insights effectively.
Experimentation Support: Support A/B testing and other experimentation frameworks by analyzing results, providing statistical significance, and recommending next steps.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner closely with Product Managers, UX/UI Designers, Engineering teams, and other business stakeholders to define key performance indicators, understand business questions, and deliver impactful analytical solutions.
Recommendations: Translate complex data findings into clear, concise, and actionable recommendations for product enhancements and strategic initiatives.

What You’ll Need to Succeed:

Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or a related discipline.
4+ years of experience working as a Product Analyst, Data Analyst, or similar role, with a strong focus on product or retail data.
Expert level proficiency in SQL for complex data querying and analysis.
Strong proficiency in Python for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and automation (e.g., using libraries like Pandas, NumPy, SciPy).
Demonstrated experience with product analytics, customer retention, churn analysis, and customer journey analytics.
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI) to create impactful dashboards and reports.
Familiarity with web analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Amplitude, Mixpanel) is a plus.
Solid understanding of statistical concepts and their application in A/B testing and data analysis.
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to articulate complex analytical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Proven ability to manage multiple stakeholders and priorities in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
Experience working in an agile product development setting.
Experience in retail or eCommerce is highly desirable.
A strong desire to learn, adapt, and continuously improve analytical methodologies and tools.




What You’ll get in Return

The client is offering a contract opportunity with possible extension.




This posting is for an existing vacancy with the organization.

AI may be used to screen, assess or select applicants for the position.

Title: Business System Analyst
Description:
Inclusion without Exception




Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an equal opportunity employer, and embraces diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability, neurodiversity, and sexual orientation, to create a workforce that reflects the societies we operate in. Our continued commitment to Culture and Diversity is reflected in our people stories across our workforce and implemented through equitable workplace policies and processes.




Tata Consultancy Services (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS) is the technology partner of choice for industry-leading organizations worldwide. Since its inception in 1968, TCS has upheld the highest standards of innovation, engineering excellence and customer service.

It has set an aspiration to become the world's largest AI-led technology services company and is enabling its clients to transform themselves across the full AI stack, from infrastructure to intelligence.




Rooted in the heritage of the Tata Group, TCS is focused on creating long term value for its clients, its investors, its employees, and the community at large. With a highly skilled workforce spread across 56 countries and 194 service delivery centers across the world, the company has been recognized as a top employer in six continents. With the ability to rapidly apply and scale new technologies, the company has built long term partnerships with its clients. Many of these relationships have endured into decades and navigated every technology cycle, from mainframes in the 1970s to artificial intelligence today.




Systems Analyst (Business Analyst)




Job Summary:

We are seeking a skilled Systems Analyst (BA) with strong experience in Capital Markets and financial systems. The ideal candidate will be responsible for requirements gathering, data analysis, and solution design while working closely with Product Controllers, Finance teams, and technical stakeholders on medium to large-scale initiatives.




Key Responsibilities:




Conduct requirements gathering sessions with Product Controllers, Finance Controllers, and stakeholders
Analyze and document business and functional requirements
Lead and facilitate workshops for requirement discovery, analysis, and validation
Understand and analyze Capital Market products such as MBS, Bonds, and Rate Derivatives
Work with trade lifecycle concepts including booking, settlement, positions, sub-ledger, and price valuation
Perform data analysis, reverse engineering, and gap analysis for existing systems
Analyze existing data models (MS Access) and align with business requirements
Identify upstream and downstream system dependencies
Create data flow diagrams, system context diagrams, and design solutions
Collaborate with development teams to ensure accurate implementation of requirements
Provide support during testing cycles and validate system outputs




Required Skills:




Strong experience in Business Analysis and Systems Analysis
In-depth knowledge of Capital Markets and financial products
Hands-on experience in data analysis and data modeling
Strong understanding of trade lifecycle and financial systems
Proficiency in SQL and working with databases (MS Access preferred)
Experience in requirement gathering, documentation, and stakeholder management
Ability to perform gap analysis and solution design
Strong communication and interpersonal skills




TCS does not use artificial intelligence tools for candidate screening or evaluation. This post is for a current vacancy. The hiring process includes an initial screening, followed by a technical evaluation and managerial discussion.




Tata Consultancy Services Canada Inc. is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) and the Ontario Human Rights Code (OHRC). Should you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please inform Human Resources.




Thank you for your interest in TCS. Candidates that meet the qualifications for this position will be contacted within a 2-week period. We invite you to continue to apply for other opportunities that match your profile.

Title: PepsiCo Canada: Fall 2026 Data Science Co-op
Description:
Overview

PepsiCo Canada: Fall 2026 Data Science Co-op

Co-op Work Term Duration: September to December 2026

Start Date: September 8, 2026

Job Location: Mississauga Headquarters

Compensation: $25 to $37.50 per hour

Overview:

At PepsiCo Canada, we have great brands, great distribution systems and great quality products, all of which are led by great people! To ensure we stay on the path of delivering great results, it is important that we continue to develop a deep bench of talented leaders. Our ability to grow year after year is driven by our ability to attract, develop, and retain world class people who will thrive in the PepsiCo environment.

Our people are our greatest asset. By supporting their ability to work effectively together and providing them with the tools they need to succeed, we are ensuring that PepsiCo Canada is the kind of company where talented people of all backgrounds want to work. The PepsiCo Canada co-op program will provide candidates with a unique opportunity to build their experience and leadership skills.

Are you excited about analytics and passionate about problem solving? Then come join our Data Science team at PepsiCo Foods Canada! We are focused on accelerating consumer demand, strengthening customer partnership, and driving end to end productivity through data science and advanced analytics. We work with Shopper Insights, Shopper Marketing, Category Leadership, Supply Chain, and IT resources to unlock opportunities. Being embedded in the business and linked to the top strategic priorities allows us to build best-in-class capabilities, develop deep functional expertise, and offer focused products in an agile responsive manner.

Help us solve new and exciting business problems with data analysis, advanced analytics, and a love of problem solving. Success in this role will require exceptional data analysis skills, an analytical mindset, intellectual curiosity, and strong appreciation for delivering business results. Strong communication and collaboration skills are required.

Responsibilities


Translate Business needs into technical requirements
Collaborating with business stakeholders and technical teams to deliver a variety of projects
Deliver advanced analytics and data science projects from end-to-end
Effectively communicating insights to project stakeholders
Complete proof of concepts
Support the adoption of solutions into the business process


Qualifications


Working towards bachelor’s degree in Data/Computer Science, Mathematics/Statistics, an engineering discipline, or equivalent.
Familiarity with statistical modeling, machine learning and/or deep learning modeling
Knowledge of statistical analysis tools like R or Python
Knowledge of Databases and SQL programming skills
Ability to translate business and technical requirements into non-technical terms
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Analytical and conceptual thinking skills
Demonstrated ability to thrive in fast paced environment
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision


The Company

PepsiCo is making a difference on a global scale, and so can you! Competing in a dynamic, fast-paced industry, our ability to grow year after year is driven by our ability to attract, develop, and retain world class people who will thrive at PepsiCo. Follow the link below to find out more about our culture and commitment to Performance with Purpose. We believe that delivering value for our consumers and customers, protecting our environment, sourcing with integrity, and investing in our employees are not simply good things to do, but that these actions fuel our returns and position PepsiCo for long-term, sustainable growth.

Grow. Belong. Smile.

AI Disclosure: PepsiCo may use Artificial Intelligence as part of its hiring process. Specifically, we may use AI-based tools to screen, assess, or select applicants.

Vacancy Status: This posting is for a newly created role.

Title: Information Security Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Information Security Analyst

Work Location: Toronto, ON- Hybrid (2 Days Onsite | 9 AM 5 PM)

Duration: 6 8 Months

Experience: 3 4 Years

Role Overview

Looking for an Information Security Analyst with strong experience in cyber security, technology risk, and compliance operations. This role involves high stakeholder interaction and focuses on monitoring, identifying, and remediating security compliance issues across enterprise systems.

Key Responsibilities


Monitor and triage reports/dashboards to identify security non-compliant assets
Create and maintain runbooks for issue tracking, notification, and escalation
Partner with technology teams to drive remediation and resolve compliance gaps
Provide technical expertise in application security practices and controls
Research, design, and implement security solutions and improvements
Lead evaluation and testing of security tools and technologies
Produce status reports and summaries on remediation progress


Required Skills


3 4 years of experience in Cyber Security / Information Security / Technology Risk
Strong understanding of Three Lines of Defence (3LOD) model
Hands-on experience with:
ServiceNow (Vulnerability Response / Compliance Operations)
Splunk (basic queries, monitoring)
Experience in Vulnerability Management / Compliance Operations
Knowledge of Risk Management frameworks
Strong communication skills (verbal & written)
Nice-to-Have Skills


Experience creating dashboards in ServiceNow
Exposure to Jira
Experience with Power BI
Familiarity with CrowdStrike
Experience in IT Audit / Technology Risk Assessment


Additional Details


High level of stakeholder interaction
Medium-sized team environment
Focus on Business-as-Usual (BAU) activities
Performance measured based on deliverables and timelines
